{{Cite sources|date=March 2017}} {{Onsen in Japan |Name = Minakami Onsen |Image = Minakami Onsen 01.jpg |Caption = {{ill|Tone River|ja|利根川}} riverside view at Minakami-kan |Location = {{ill|Minakami|ja|みなかみ町}}, Tone District, Gunma Prefecture | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 45 | Latitude seconds = 59 | Longitude degrees = 138 | Longitude minutes = 57 | Longitude seconds = 29 |Access = Train: {{ill|Jōetsu Line|ja|上越線}}, alight at Minakami Station, approximately 5 minutes on foot
Car: Exit at {{ill|Minakami Interchange|ja|水上インターチェンジ}} on {{ill|Kan-Etsu Expressway|ja|関越自動車道}}, follow National Route 291 |Hot spring type = {{ill|Sulfate spring|ja|硫酸塩泉|1=Sulfate spring}} |Flow rate = |pH = |Classification of fluid = |Classification of osmotic pressure = |Number of accommodations = |Total capacity = |Annual bathers = |Year of statistics = |External link = http://www.minakamionsen.com/ |Remarks = }} '''Minakami Onsen''' (水上温泉) is an onsen located in {{ill|Minakami|ja|みなかみ町}}, Tone District, Gunma Prefecture. The area south of {{ill|Mount Tanigawa|ja|谷川岳}} is known as {{ill|Minakami Onsenkyo|ja|水上温泉郷}}, with Minakami Onsen serving as its central hub. Following {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, its excellent {{ill|transportation access|ja|交通アクセス}} facilitated rapid expansion, establishing it as one of the major onsen areas in the prefecture alongside {{ill|Kusatsu Onsen|ja|草津温泉}} and {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉}}. In the {{ill|Jomo Karuta|ja|上毛かるた}}, it is celebrated as "Mi no fuda: Minakami-Tanigawa, skiing and climbing." == Hot Spring Properties == * Calcium - {{ill|Sulfate spring|ja|硫酸塩泉|1=Sulfate spring}} == Onsen Town == The onsen town clusters around {{ill|Minakami Station|ja|水上駅}} on the {{ill|Jōetsu Line|ja|上越線}}, with hotels and inns densely packed along the steep banks of the upper {{ill|Tone River|ja|利根川}}. The area is dotted with scenic spots such as {{ill|Suwa Gorge|ja|諏訪峡}} and Minakami Gorge, attracting numerous visitors during the autumn foliage season. The slow-moving {{ill|Horse-drawn carriage|ja|トテ馬車}} rides are a signature attraction of Minakami Onsen. Bridges over the Tone River, such as Yuhara Bridge and Minakami Bridge, are known for their nighttime illumination. Traditional amusements, including {{ill|Smart ball|ja|スマートボール}} parlors, evoke nostalgia and draw modern visitors. Despite its focus on overnight stays, day-use facilities are limited but increasing. Many inns now accommodate day-use visitors. Public facilities include the town-run "Yuterume Tanigawa" and the "Fureai Koryukan," which offers foot baths. == History == According to legend, Minakami Onsen was founded during the {{ill|Eiroku era|ja|永禄年間}} when the monk Kaiō discovered steam rising from a cliff along the Tone River. Before the opening of the {{ill|Shimizu Tunnel|ja|清水トンネル}}, the area was a remote mountain retreat. Writers like {{ill|Bokusui Wakayama|ja|若山牧水}}, {{ill|Osamu Dazai|ja|太宰治}}, {{ill|Hakushū Kitahara|ja|北原白秋}}, and {{ill|Akiko Yosano|ja|与謝野晶子}} were captivated by the harmony of hot springs and dramatic valleys. The area was then known as Yuhara Onsen, a name still in occasional use. The opening of the {{ill|Jōetsu Line|ja|上越線}} in 1931 brought Minakami Onsen closer to the {{ill|Greater Tokyo Area|ja|首都圏}}. The 1966 {{ill|Kikufuji Hotel fire|ja|菊富士ホテル火災}} marked a significant event in its history. The construction of the {{ill|Kan-Etsu Expressway|ja|関越自動車道}} and the introduction of express trains like the {{ill|Yukemuri Express|ja|水上列車}} led to its rise as a major recreation destination. However, the shift towards group tours and nightlife transformed the onsen into a bustling entertainment hub, complete with bars and show pubs. Following the {{ill|economic bubble collapse|ja|バブル崩壊}}, declining group tours and changing tourist preferences forced the onsen to refocus. Today, it aims to leverage its natural surroundings, integrating {{ill|skiing|ja|スキー}}, {{ill|rafting|ja|ラフティング}}, and other outdoor activities to develop as a recreational resort. == Recent Issues == The {{ill|Ōshimizu Tunnel|ja|大清水トンネル}} of the {{ill|Jōetsu Shinkansen|ja|上越新幹線}} passes 50 meters beneath the hot spring's aquifers, causing decreased water flow. In 1979, the issue was addressed in the {{ill|House of Representatives of Japan|ja|衆議院}}, prompting the {{ill|Japan Railway Construction Public Corporation|ja|日本鉄道建設公団}} to drill new wells. The 2004 {{ill|onsen fraud scandal|ja|温泉偽装問題}} revealed that some facilities used tap water while advertising natural hot springs, raising concerns over misrepresentation and improper tax collection. The {{ill|abandoned Hotel Ōmiya|ja|ホテル大宮}}, located near Minakami Station, remains an unresolved issue for the local community. == Access == * Train: {{ill|Jōetsu Line|ja|上越線}}, Minakami Station, approximately 5 minutes on foot * Car: Exit at Minakami Interchange on the {{ill|Kan-Etsu Expressway|ja|関越自動車道}}, via National Route 291 == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * ''Disappearing Ruins of Japan: Hidden History Through Ruins,'' Taiyo Publishing, 2014 == See Also == * {{ill|Ii Yudana|ja|いい湯だな}} (song by {{ill|The Dukes Aces|ja|デューク・エイセス}}) - Features Minakami in the fourth verse == External Links == * [https://www.enjoy-minakami.jp/ Minakami Perfect Guide] - Official tourism website of Minakami {{Onsen}} [[Category:Onsen in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Minakami]] [[Category:Minakami Onsen]][[Category:Minakami Onsen]][[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:Minakami Onsen]] [[Category:Minakami Onsen]] [[Category:Sulfate Spring]] [[Category:Copper-Iron Spring]] [[ja:水上温泉]] [[Category:Pages of uncertain originality]]

Car: Exit at {{ill|Minakami Interchange|ja|水上インターチェンジ}} on {{ill|Kan-Etsu Expressway|ja|関越自動車道}}, follow National Route 291 |Hot spring type = {{ill|Sulfate spring|ja|硫酸塩泉|1=Sulfate spring}} |Flow rate = |pH = |Classification of fluid = |Classification of osmotic pressure = |Number of accommodations = |Total capacity = |Annual bathers = |Year of statistics = |External link = http://www.minakamionsen.com/ |Remarks = }} '''Minakami Onsen''' (水上温泉) is an onsen located in {{ill|Minakami|ja|みなかみ町}}, Tone District, Gunma Prefecture. The area south of {{ill|Mount Tanigawa|ja|谷川岳}} is known as {{ill|Minakami Onsenkyo|ja|水上温泉郷}}, with Minakami Onsen serving as its central hub. Following {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, its excellent {{ill|transportation access|ja|交通アクセス}} facilitated rapid expansion, establishing it as one of the major onsen areas in the prefecture alongside {{ill|Kusatsu Onsen|ja|草津温泉}} and {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉}}. In the {{ill|Jomo Karuta|ja|上毛かるた}}, it is celebrated as "Mi no fuda: Minakami-Tanigawa, skiing and climbing." == Hot Spring Properties == * Calcium - {{ill|Sulfate spring|ja|硫酸塩泉|1=Sulfate spring}} == Onsen Town == The onsen town clusters around {{ill|Minakami Station|ja|水上駅}} on the {{ill|Jōetsu Line|ja|上越線}}, with hotels and inns densely packed along the steep banks of the upper {{ill|Tone River|ja|利根川}}. The area is dotted with scenic spots such as {{ill|Suwa Gorge|ja|諏訪峡}} and Minakami Gorge, attracting numerous visitors during the autumn foliage season. The slow-moving {{ill|Horse-drawn carriage|ja|トテ馬車}} rides are a signature attraction of Minakami Onsen. Bridges over the Tone River, such as Yuhara Bridge and Minakami Bridge, are known for their nighttime illumination. Traditional amusements, including {{ill|Smart ball|ja|スマートボール}} parlors, evoke nostalgia and draw modern visitors. Despite its focus on overnight stays, day-use facilities are limited but increasing. Many inns now accommodate day-use visitors. Public facilities include the town-run "Yuterume Tanigawa" and the "Fureai Koryukan," which offers foot baths. == History == According to legend, Minakami Onsen was founded during the {{ill|Eiroku era|ja|永禄年間}} when the monk Kaiō discovered steam rising from a cliff along the Tone River. Before the opening of the {{ill|Shimizu Tunnel|ja|清水トンネル}}, the area was a remote mountain retreat. Writers like {{ill|Bokusui Wakayama|ja|若山牧水}}, {{ill|Osamu Dazai|ja|太宰治}}, {{ill|Hakushū Kitahara|ja|北原白秋}}, and {{ill|Akiko Yosano|ja|与謝野晶子}} were captivated by the harmony of hot springs and dramatic valleys. The area was then known as Yuhara Onsen, a name still in occasional use. The opening of the {{ill|Jōetsu Line|ja|上越線}} in 1931 brought Minakami Onsen closer to the {{ill|Greater Tokyo Area|ja|首都圏}}. The 1966 {{ill|Kikufuji Hotel fire|ja|菊富士ホテル火災}} marked a significant event in its history. The construction of the {{ill|Kan-Etsu Expressway|ja|関越自動車道}} and the introduction of express trains like the {{ill|Yukemuri Express|ja|水上列車}} led to its rise as a major recreation destination. However, the shift towards group tours and nightlife transformed the onsen into a bustling entertainment hub, complete with bars and show pubs. Following the {{ill|economic bubble collapse|ja|バブル崩壊}}, declining group tours and changing tourist preferences forced the onsen to refocus. Today, it aims to leverage its natural surroundings, integrating {{ill|skiing|ja|スキー}}, {{ill|rafting|ja|ラフティング}}, and other outdoor activities to develop as a recreational resort. == Recent Issues == The {{ill|Ōshimizu Tunnel|ja|大清水トンネル}} of the {{ill|Jōetsu Shinkansen|ja|上越新幹線}} passes 50 meters beneath the hot spring's aquifers, causing decreased water flow. In 1979, the issue was addressed in the {{ill|House of Representatives of Japan|ja|衆議院}}, prompting the {{ill|Japan Railway Construction Public Corporation|ja|日本鉄道建設公団}} to drill new wells. The 2004 {{ill|onsen fraud scandal|ja|温泉偽装問題|1=Onsen Fraud Scandal}} revealed that some facilities used tap water while advertising natural hot springs, raising concerns over misrepresentation and improper tax collection. The {{ill|abandoned Hotel Ōmiya|ja|ホテル大宮}}, located near Minakami Station, remains an unresolved issue for the local community. == Access == * Train: {{ill|Jōetsu Line|ja|上越線}}, Minakami Station, approximately 5 minutes on foot * Car: Exit at Minakami Interchange on the {{ill|Kan-Etsu Expressway|ja|関越自動車道}}, via National Route 291 == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * ''Disappearing Ruins of Japan: Hidden History Through Ruins,'' Taiyo Publishing, 2014 == See Also == * {{ill|Ii Yudana|ja|いい湯だな}} (song by {{ill|The Dukes Aces|ja|デューク・エイセス}}) - Features Minakami in the fourth verse == External Links == * [https://www.enjoy-minakami.jp/ Minakami Perfect Guide] - Official tourism website of Minakami {{Onsen}} [[Category:Onsen in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Minakami]] [[Category:Minakami Onsen]][[Category:Minakami Onsen]][[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:Minakami Onsen]] [[Category:Minakami Onsen]] [[Category:Sulfate Spring]] [[Category:Copper-Iron Spring]] [[ja:水上温泉]] [[Category:Pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:11]][[en:Minakami Onsen]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]]

The area south of {{ill|Mount Tanigawa|ja|谷川岳}} is known as {{ill|Minakami Onsenkyo|ja|水上温泉郷}}, with Minakami Onsen serving as its central hub. Following {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, its excellent {{ill|transportation access|ja|交通アクセス}} facilitated rapid expansion, establishing it as one of the major onsen areas in the prefecture alongside {{ill|Kusatsu Onsen|ja|草津温泉}} and {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉}}. In the {{ill|Jomo Karuta|ja|上毛かるた}}, it is celebrated as "Mi no fuda: Minakami-Tanigawa, skiing and climbing." == Hot Spring Properties == * Calcium - {{ill|Sulfate spring|ja|硫酸塩泉|1=Sulfate spring}} == Onsen Town == The onsen town clusters around {{ill|Minakami Station|ja|水上駅}} on the {{ill|Jōetsu Line|ja|上越線}}, with hotels and inns densely packed along the steep banks of the upper {{ill|Tone River|ja|利根川}}. The area is dotted with scenic spots such as {{ill|Suwa Gorge|ja|諏訪峡}} and Minakami Gorge, attracting numerous visitors during the autumn foliage season. The slow-moving {{ill|Horse-drawn carriage|ja|トテ馬車}} rides are a signature attraction of Minakami Onsen. Bridges over the Tone River, such as Yuhara Bridge and Minakami Bridge, are known for their nighttime illumination. Traditional amusements, including {{ill|Smart ball|ja|スマートボール}} parlors, evoke nostalgia and draw modern visitors. Despite its focus on overnight stays, day-use facilities are limited but increasing. Many inns now accommodate day-use visitors. Public facilities include the town-run "Yuterume Tanigawa" and the "Fureai Koryukan," which offers foot baths. == History == According to legend, Minakami Onsen was founded during the {{ill|Eiroku era|ja|永禄年間}} when the monk Kaiō discovered steam rising from a cliff along the Tone River. Before the opening of the {{ill|Shimizu Tunnel|ja|清水トンネル}}, the area was a remote mountain retreat. Writers like {{ill|Bokusui Wakayama|ja|若山牧水}}, {{ill|Osamu Dazai|ja|太宰治}}, {{ill|Hakushū Kitahara|ja|北原白秋}}, and {{ill|Akiko Yosano|ja|与謝野晶子}} were captivated by the harmony of hot springs and dramatic valleys. The area was then known as Yuhara Onsen, a name still in occasional use. The opening of the {{ill|Jōetsu Line|ja|上越線}} in 1931 brought Minakami Onsen closer to the {{ill|Greater Tokyo Area|ja|首都圏}}. The 1966 {{ill|Kikufuji Hotel fire|ja|菊富士ホテル火災}} marked a significant event in its history. The construction of the {{ill|Kan-Etsu Expressway|ja|関越自動車道}} and the introduction of express trains like the {{ill|Yukemuri Express|ja|水上列車}} led to its rise as a major recreation destination. However, the shift towards group tours and nightlife transformed the onsen into a bustling entertainment hub, complete with bars and show pubs. Following the {{ill|economic bubble collapse|ja|バブル崩壊}}, declining group tours and changing tourist preferences forced the onsen to refocus. Today, it aims to leverage its natural surroundings, integrating {{ill|skiing|ja|スキー}}, {{ill|rafting|ja|ラフティング}}, and other outdoor activities to develop as a recreational resort. == Recent Issues == The {{ill|Ōshimizu Tunnel|ja|大清水トンネル}} of the {{ill|Jōetsu Shinkansen|ja|上越新幹線}} passes 50 meters beneath the hot spring's aquifers, causing decreased water flow. In 1979, the issue was addressed in the {{ill|House of Representatives of Japan|ja|衆議院}}, prompting the {{ill|Japan Railway Construction Public Corporation|ja|日本鉄道建設公団}} to drill new wells. The 2004 {{ill|onsen fraud scandal|ja|温泉偽装問題|1=Onsen Fraud Scandal}} revealed that some facilities used tap water while advertising natural hot springs, raising concerns over misrepresentation and improper tax collection. The {{ill|abandoned Hotel Ōmiya|ja|ホテル大宮}}, located near Minakami Station, remains an unresolved issue for the local community. == Access == * Train: {{ill|Jōetsu Line|ja|上越線}}, Minakami Station, approximately 5 minutes on foot * Car: Exit at Minakami Interchange on the {{ill|Kan-Etsu Expressway|ja|関越自動車道}}, via National Route 291 == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * ''Disappearing Ruins of Japan: Hidden History Through Ruins,'' Taiyo Publishing, 2014 == See Also == * {{ill|Ii Yudana|ja|いい湯だな}} (song by {{ill|The Dukes Aces|ja|デューク・エイセス}}) - Features Minakami in the fourth verse == External Links == * [https://www.enjoy-minakami.jp/ Minakami Perfect Guide] - Official tourism website of Minakami [[Category:Onsen in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Minakami]] [[Category:Minakami Onsen]][[Category:Minakami Onsen]][[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:Minakami Onsen]] [[Category:Minakami Onsen]] [[Category:Sulfate Spring]] [[Category:Copper-Iron Spring]] [[ja:水上温泉]] [[Category:Pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:11]][[en:Minakami Onsen]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Minakami Onsen}}{{citation needed|Minakami Onsen}}{{Onsen}}

In the {{ill|Jomo Karuta|ja|上毛かるた}}, it is celebrated as "Mi no fuda: Minakami-Tanigawa, skiing and climbing." == Hot Spring Properties == * Calcium - {{ill|Sulfate spring|ja|硫酸塩泉|1=Sulfate spring}} == Onsen Town == The onsen town clusters around {{ill|Minakami Station|ja|水上駅}} on the {{ill|Jōetsu Line|ja|上越線}}, with hotels and inns densely packed along the steep banks of the upper {{ill|Tone River|ja|利根川}}. The area is dotted with scenic spots such as {{ill|Suwa Gorge|ja|諏訪峡}} and Minakami Gorge, attracting numerous visitors during the autumn foliage season. The slow-moving {{ill|Horse-drawn carriage|ja|トテ馬車}} rides are a signature attraction of Minakami Onsen. Bridges over the Tone River, such as Yuhara Bridge and Minakami Bridge, are known for their nighttime illumination. Traditional amusements, including {{ill|Smart ball|ja|スマートボール}} parlors, evoke nostalgia and draw modern visitors. Despite its focus on overnight stays, day-use facilities are limited but increasing. Many inns now accommodate day-use visitors. Public facilities include the town-run "Yuterume Tanigawa" and the "Fureai Koryukan," which offers foot baths. == History == According to legend, Minakami Onsen was founded during the {{ill|Eiroku era|ja|永禄年間}} when the monk Kaiō discovered steam rising from a cliff along the Tone River. Before the opening of the {{ill|Shimizu Tunnel|ja|清水トンネル}}, the area was a remote mountain retreat. Writers like {{ill|Bokusui Wakayama|ja|若山牧水}}, {{ill|Osamu Dazai|ja|太宰治}}, {{ill|Hakushū Kitahara|ja|北原白秋}}, and {{ill|Akiko Yosano|ja|与謝野晶子}} were captivated by the harmony of hot springs and dramatic valleys. The area was then known as Yuhara Onsen, a name still in occasional use. The opening of the {{ill|Jōetsu Line|ja|上越線}} in 1931 brought Minakami Onsen closer to the {{ill|Greater Tokyo Area|ja|首都圏}}. The 1966 {{ill|Kikufuji Hotel fire|ja|菊富士ホテル火災}} marked a significant event in its history. The construction of the {{ill|Kan-Etsu Expressway|ja|関越自動車道}} and the introduction of express trains like the {{ill|Yukemuri Express|ja|水上列車}} led to its rise as a major recreation destination. However, the shift towards group tours and nightlife transformed the onsen into a bustling entertainment hub, complete with bars and show pubs. Following the {{ill|economic bubble collapse|ja|バブル崩壊}}, declining group tours and changing tourist preferences forced the onsen to refocus. Today, it aims to leverage its natural surroundings, integrating {{ill|skiing|ja|スキー}}, {{ill|rafting|ja|ラフティング}}, and other outdoor activities to develop as a recreational resort. == Recent Issues == The {{ill|Ōshimizu Tunnel|ja|大清水トンネル}} of the {{ill|Jōetsu Shinkansen|ja|上越新幹線}} passes 50 meters beneath the hot spring's aquifers, causing decreased water flow. In 1979, the issue was addressed in the {{ill|House of Representatives of Japan|ja|衆議院}}, prompting the {{ill|Japan Railway Construction Public Corporation|ja|日本鉄道建設公団}} to drill new wells. The 2004 {{ill|onsen fraud scandal|ja|温泉偽装問題|1=Onsen Fraud Scandal}} revealed that some facilities used tap water while advertising natural hot springs, raising concerns over misrepresentation and improper tax collection. The {{ill|abandoned Hotel Ōmiya|ja|ホテル大宮}}, located near Minakami Station, remains an unresolved issue for the local community. == Access == * Train: {{ill|Jōetsu Line|ja|上越線}}, Minakami Station, approximately 5 minutes on foot * Car: Exit at Minakami Interchange on the {{ill|Kan-Etsu Expressway|ja|関越自動車道}}, via National Route 291 == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * ''Disappearing Ruins of Japan: Hidden History Through Ruins,'' Taiyo Publishing, 2014 == See Also! == * {{ill|Ii Yudana|ja|いい湯だな}} (song by {{ill|The Dukes Aces|ja|デューク・エイセス}}) - Features Minakami in the fourth verse == External Links == * [https://www.enjoy-minakami.jp/ Minakami Perfect Guide] - Official tourism website of Minakami [[Category:Onsen in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Minakami]] [[Category:Minakami Onsen]][[Category:Minakami Onsen]][[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:Minakami Onsen]] [[Category:Minakami Onsen]] [[Category:Sulfate Spring]] [[Category:Copper-Iron Spring]] [[ja:水上温泉]] [[Category:Pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:11]][[en:Minakami Onsen]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Minakami Onsen}}{{citation needed|Minakami Onsen}} {{Onsen}}

The area south of {{ill|Mount Tanigawa|ja|谷川岳|12=draft}} is known as {{ill|Minakami Onsenkyo|ja|水上温泉郷|12=draft}}, with Minakami Onsen serving as its central hub. Following {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦|12=draft}}, its excellent {{ill|transportation access|ja|交通アクセス|12=draft}} facilitated rapid expansion, establishing it as one of the major onsen areas in the prefecture alongside {{ill|Kusatsu Onsen|ja|草津温泉|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉|12=draft}}. In the {{ill|Jomo Karuta|ja|上毛かるた|12=draft}}, it is celebrated as "Mi no fuda: Minakami-Tanigawa, skiing and climbing." == Hot Spring Properties == * Calcium - {{ill|Sulfate spring|ja|硫酸塩泉|1=Sulfate spring|12=draft}} == Onsen Town == The onsen town clusters around {{ill|Minakami Station|ja|水上駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Jōetsu Line|ja|上越線|12=draft}}, with hotels and inns densely packed along the steep banks of the upper {{ill|Tone River|ja|利根川|12=draft}}. The area is dotted with scenic spots such as {{ill|Suwa Gorge|ja|諏訪峡|12=draft}} and Minakami Gorge, attracting numerous visitors during the autumn foliage season. The slow-moving {{ill|Horse-drawn carriage|ja|トテ馬車|12=draft}} rides are a signature attraction of Minakami Onsen. Bridges over the Tone River, such as Yuhara Bridge and Minakami Bridge, are known for their nighttime illumination. Traditional amusements, including {{ill|Smart ball|ja|スマートボール|12=draft}} parlors, evoke nostalgia and draw modern visitors. Despite its focus on overnight stays, day-use facilities are limited but increasing. Many inns now accommodate day-use visitors. Public facilities include the town-run "Yuterume Tanigawa" and the "Fureai Koryukan," which offers foot baths. == History == According to legend, Minakami Onsen was founded during the {{ill|Eiroku era|ja|永禄年間|12=draft}} when the monk Kaiō discovered steam rising from a cliff along the Tone River. Before the opening of the {{ill|Shimizu Tunnel|ja|清水トンネル|12=draft}}, the area was a remote mountain retreat. Writers like {{ill|Bokusui Wakayama|ja|若山牧水|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osamu Dazai|ja|太宰治|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hakushū Kitahara|ja|北原白秋|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Akiko Yosano|ja|与謝野晶子|12=draft}} were captivated by the harmony of hot springs and dramatic valleys. The area was then known as Yuhara Onsen, a name still in occasional use. The opening of the {{ill|Jōetsu Line|ja|上越線|12=draft}} in 1931 brought Minakami Onsen closer to the {{ill|Greater Tokyo Area|ja|首都圏|12=draft}}. The 1966 {{ill|Kikufuji Hotel fire|ja|菊富士ホテル火災|12=draft}} marked a significant event in its history. The construction of the {{ill|Kan-Etsu Expressway|ja|関越自動車道|12=draft}} and the introduction of express trains like the {{ill|Yukemuri Express|ja|水上列車|12=draft}} led to its rise as a major recreation destination. However, the shift towards group tours and nightlife transformed the onsen into a bustling entertainment hub, complete with bars and show pubs. Following the {{ill|economic bubble collapse|ja|バブル崩壊|12=draft}}, declining group tours and changing tourist preferences forced the onsen to refocus. Today, it aims to leverage its natural surroundings, integrating {{ill|skiing|ja|スキー|12=draft}}, {{ill|rafting|ja|ラフティング|12=draft}}, and other outdoor activities to develop as a recreational resort. == Recent Issues == The {{ill|Ōshimizu Tunnel|ja|大清水トンネル|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Jōetsu Shinkansen|ja|上越新幹線|12=draft}} passes 50 meters beneath the hot spring's aquifers, causing decreased water flow. In 1979, the issue was addressed in the {{ill|House of Representatives of Japan|ja|衆議院|12=draft}}, prompting the {{ill|Japan Railway Construction Public Corporation|ja|日本鉄道建設公団|12=draft}} to drill new wells. The 2004 {{ill|onsen fraud scandal|ja|温泉偽装問題|1=Onsen Fraud Scandal|12=draft}} revealed that some facilities used tap water while advertising natural hot springs, raising concerns over misrepresentation and improper tax collection. The {{ill|abandoned Hotel Ōmiya|ja|ホテル大宮|12=draft}}, located near Minakami Station, remains an unresolved issue for the local community. == Access == * Train: {{ill|Jōetsu Line|ja|上越線|12=draft}}, Minakami Station, approximately 5 minutes on foot * Car: Exit at Minakami Interchange on the {{ill|Kan-Etsu Expressway|ja|関越自動車道|12=draft}}, via National Route 291 == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * ''Disappearing Ruins of Japan: Hidden History Through Ruins,'' Taiyo Publishing, 2014 == See Also == * {{ill|Ii Yudana|ja|いい湯だな|12=draft}} (song by {{ill|The Dukes Aces|ja|デューク・エイセス|12=draft}}) - Features Minakami in the fourth verse == External Links == * [https://www.enjoy-minakami.jp/ Minakami Perfect Guide] - Official tourism website of Minakami {{citation needed|Minakami Onsen}} {{Onsen}} {{Onsen}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Onsen in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Minakami]] [[Category:Minakami Onsen]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:Sulfate Spring]] [[Category:Copper-Iron Spring]] [[Category:Pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[ja:水上温泉]] [[en:Minakami Onsen]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Minakami Onsen}}

{{Cite sources|date=March 2017}} {{Onsen in Japan |Name = Minakami Onsen |image = Minakami Onsen 01.jpg |Caption = {{ill|Tone River|ja|利根川|12=draft}} riverside view at Minakami-kan |Location = {{ill|Minakami|ja|みなかみ町|12=draft}}, Tone District, Gunma Prefecture | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 45 | Latitude seconds = 59 | Longitude degrees = 138 | Longitude minutes = 57 | Longitude seconds = 29 |Access = Train: {{ill|Jōetsu Line|ja|上越線|12=draft}}, alight at Minakami Station, approximately 5 minutes on foot
Car: Exit at {{ill|Minakami Interchange|ja|水上インターチェンジ|12=draft}} on {{ill|Kan-Etsu Expressway|ja|関越自動車道|12=draft}}, follow National Route 291 |Hot spring type = {{ill|Sulfate spring|ja|硫酸塩泉|1=Sulfate spring|12=draft}} |Flow rate = |pH = |Classification of fluid = |Classification of osmotic pressure = |Number of accommodations = |Total capacity = |Annual bathers = |Year of statistics = |External link = http://www.minakamionsen.com/ |Remarks = }} '''Minakami Onsen''' (水上温泉) is an onsen located in {{ill|Minakami|ja|みなかみ町|12=draft}}, Tone District, Gunma Prefecture. The area south of {{ill|Mount Tanigawa|ja|谷川岳|12=draft}} is known as {{ill|Minakami Onsenkyo|ja|水上温泉郷|12=draft}}, with Minakami Onsen serving as its central hub. Following {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦|12=draft}}, its excellent {{ill|transportation access|ja|交通アクセス|12=draft}} facilitated rapid expansion, establishing it as one of the major onsen areas in the prefecture alongside {{ill|Kusatsu Onsen|ja|草津温泉|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉|12=draft}}. In the {{ill|Jomo Karuta|ja|上毛かるた|12=draft}}, it is celebrated as "Mi no fuda: Minakami-Tanigawa, skiing and climbing." == Hot Spring Properties == * Calcium - {{ill|Sulfate spring|ja|硫酸塩泉|1=Sulfate spring|12=draft}} == Onsen Town == The onsen town clusters around {{ill|Minakami Station|ja|水上駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Jōetsu Line|ja|上越線|12=draft}}, with hotels and inns densely packed along the steep banks of the upper {{ill|Tone River|ja|利根川|12=draft}}. The area is dotted with scenic spots such as {{ill|Suwa Gorge|ja|諏訪峡|12=draft}} and Minakami Gorge, attracting numerous visitors during the autumn foliage season. The slow-moving {{ill|Horse-drawn carriage|ja|トテ馬車|12=draft}} rides are a signature attraction of Minakami Onsen. Bridges over the Tone River, such as Yuhara Bridge and Minakami Bridge, are known for their nighttime illumination. Traditional amusements, including {{ill|Smart ball|ja|スマートボール|12=draft}} parlors, evoke nostalgia and draw modern visitors. Despite its focus on overnight stays, day-use facilities are limited but increasing. Many inns now accommodate day-use visitors. Public facilities include the town-run "Yuterume Tanigawa" and the "Fureai Koryukan," which offers foot baths. == History == According to legend, Minakami Onsen was founded during the {{ill|Eiroku era|ja|永禄年間|12=draft}} when the monk Kaiō discovered steam rising from a cliff along the Tone River. Before the opening of the {{ill|Shimizu Tunnel|ja|清水トンネル|12=draft}}, the area was a remote mountain retreat. Writers like {{ill|Bokusui Wakayama|ja|若山牧水|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osamu Dazai|ja|太宰治|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hakushū Kitahara|ja|北原白秋|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Akiko Yosano|ja|与謝野晶子|12=draft}} were captivated by the harmony of hot springs and dramatic valleys. The area was then known as Yuhara Onsen, a name still in occasional use. The opening of the {{ill|Jōetsu Line|ja|上越線|12=draft}} in 1931 brought Minakami Onsen closer to the {{ill|Greater Tokyo Area|ja|首都圏|12=draft}}. The 1966 {{ill|Kikufuji Hotel fire|ja|菊富士ホテル火災|12=draft}} marked a significant event in its history. The construction of the {{ill|Kan-Etsu Expressway|ja|関越自動車道|12=draft}} and the introduction of express trains like the {{ill|Yukemuri Express|ja|水上列車|12=draft}} led to its rise as a major recreation destination. However, the shift towards group tours and nightlife transformed the onsen into a bustling entertainment hub, complete with bars and show pubs. Following the {{ill|economic bubble collapse|ja|バブル崩壊|12=draft}}, declining group tours and changing tourist preferences forced the onsen to refocus. Today, it aims to leverage its natural surroundings, integrating {{ill|skiing|ja|スキー|12=draft}}, {{ill|rafting|ja|ラフティング|12=draft}}, and other outdoor activities to develop as a recreational resort. == Recent Issues == The {{ill|Ōshimizu Tunnel|ja|大清水トンネル|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Jōetsu Shinkansen|ja|上越新幹線|12=draft}} passes 50 meters beneath the hot spring's aquifers, causing decreased water flow. In 1979, the issue was addressed in the {{ill|House of Representatives of Japan|ja|衆議院|12=draft}}, prompting the {{ill|Japan Railway Construction Public Corporation|ja|日本鉄道建設公団|12=draft}} to drill new wells. The 2004 {{ill|onsen fraud scandal|ja|温泉偽装問題|1=Onsen Fraud Scandal|12=draft}} revealed that some facilities used tap water while advertising natural hot springs, raising concerns over misrepresentation and improper tax collection. The {{ill|abandoned Hotel Ōmiya|ja|ホテル大宮|12=draft}}, located near Minakami Station, remains an unresolved issue for the local community. == Access == * Train: {{ill|Jōetsu Line|ja|上越線|12=draft}}, Minakami Station, approximately 5 minutes on foot * Car: Exit at Minakami Interchange on the {{ill|Kan-Etsu Expressway|ja|関越自動車道|12=draft}}, via National Route 291 == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * ''Disappearing Ruins of Japan: Hidden History Through Ruins,'' Taiyo Publishing, 2014 == See Also == * {{ill|Ii Yudana|ja|いい湯だな|12=draft}} (song by {{ill|The Dukes Aces|ja|デューク・エイセス|12=draft}}) - Features Minakami in the fourth verse == External Links == * [https://www.enjoy-minakami.jp/ Minakami Perfect Guide] - Official tourism website of Minakami {{citation needed|Minakami Onsen}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Onsen in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Minakami]] [[Category:Minakami Onsen]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:Sulfate Spring]] [[Category:Copper-Iron Spring]] [[Category:Pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[ja:水上温泉]] [[en:Minakami Onsen]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Minakami Onsen}} {{Onsen}}

The construction of the {{ill|Kan-Etsu Expressway|ja|関越自動車道|12=draft}} and the introduction of express trains like the {{ill|Yukemuri Express|ja|水上列車|12=draft}} led to its rise as a major recreation destination. However, the shift towards group tours and nightlife transformed the onsen into a bustling entertainment hub, complete with bars and show pubs. Following the {{ill|economic bubble collapse|ja|バブル崩壊|12=draft}}, declining group tours and changing tourist preferences forced the onsen to refocus. Today, it aims to leverage its natural surroundings, integrating {{ill|skiing|ja|スキー|12=draft}}, {{ill|rafting|ja|ラフティング|12=draft}}, and other outdoor activities to develop as a recreational resort. == Recent Issues == The {{ill|Ōshimizu Tunnel|ja|大清水トンネル|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Jōetsu Shinkansen|ja|上越新幹線|12=draft}} passes 50 meters beneath the hot spring's aquifers, causing decreased water flow. In 1979, the issue was addressed in the {{ill|House of Representatives of Japan|ja|衆議院|12=draft}}, prompting the {{ill|Japan Railway Construction Public Corporation|ja|日本鉄道建設公団|12=draft}} to drill new wells. The 2004 {{ill|onsen fraud scandal|ja|温泉偽装問題|1=Onsen Fraud Controversy|1=Onsen Fraud Scandal|12=draft}} revealed that some facilities used tap water while advertising natural hot springs, raising concerns over misrepresentation and improper tax collection. The {{ill|abandoned Hotel Ōmiya|ja|ホテル大宮|12=draft}}, located near Minakami Station, remains an unresolved issue for the local community. == Access == * Train: {{ill|Jōetsu Line|ja|上越線|12=draft}}, Minakami Station, approximately 5 minutes on foot * Car: Exit at Minakami Interchange on the {{ill|Kan-Etsu Expressway|ja|関越自動車道|12=draft}}, via National Route 291 == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * ''Disappearing Ruins of Japan: Hidden History Through Ruins,'' Taiyo Publishing, 2014 == See Also == * {{ill|Ii Yudana|ja|いい湯だな|12=draft}} (song by {{ill|The Dukes Aces|ja|デューク・エイセス|12=draft}}) - Features Minakami in the fourth verse == External Links == * [https://www.enjoy-minakami.jp/ Minakami Perfect Guide] - Official tourism website of Minakami {{citation needed|Minakami Onsen}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Onsen in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Minakami]] [[Category:Minakami Onsen]] [[Category:Sulfate Spring]] [[Category:Copper-Iron Spring]] [[Category:Pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:水上温泉]] [[en:Minakami Onsen]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Minakami Onsen}} {{Onsen}}

revision.text:
{{Cite sources|date=March 2017}} {{Onsen in Japan |Name = Minakami Onsen |image = Minakami Onsen 01.jpg |Caption = {{ill|Tone River|ja|利根川|12=draft}} riverside view at Minakami-kan |Location = {{ill|Minakami|ja|みなかみ町|12=draft}}, Tone District, Gunma Prefecture | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 45 | Latitude seconds = 59 | Longitude degrees = 138 | Longitude minutes = 57 | Longitude seconds = 29 |Access = Train: {{ill|Jōetsu Line|ja|上越線|12=draft}}, alight at Minakami Station, approximately 5 minutes on foot
Car: Exit at {{ill|Minakami Interchange|ja|水上インターチェンジ|12=draft}} on {{ill|Kan-Etsu Expressway|ja|関越自動車道|12=draft}}, follow National Route 291 |Hot spring type = {{ill|Sulfate spring|ja|硫酸塩泉|1=Sulfate spring|12=draft}} |Flow rate = |pH = |Classification of fluid = |Classification of osmotic pressure = |Number of accommodations = |Total capacity = |Annual bathers = |Year of statistics = |External link = http://www.minakamionsen.com/ |Remarks = }} '''Minakami Onsen''' (水上温泉) is an onsen located in {{ill|Minakami|ja|みなかみ町|12=draft}}, Tone District, Gunma Prefecture. The area south of {{ill|Mount Tanigawa|ja|谷川岳|12=draft}} is known as {{ill|Minakami Onsenkyo|ja|水上温泉郷|12=draft}}, with Minakami Onsen serving as its central hub. Following {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦|12=draft}}, its excellent {{ill|transportation access|ja|交通アクセス|12=draft}} facilitated rapid expansion, establishing it as one of the major onsen areas in the prefecture alongside {{ill|Kusatsu Onsen|ja|草津温泉|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉|12=draft}}. In the {{ill|Jomo Karuta|ja|上毛かるた|12=draft}}, it is celebrated as "Mi no fuda: Minakami-Tanigawa, skiing and climbing." == Hot Spring Properties == * Calcium - {{ill|Sulfate spring|ja|硫酸塩泉|1=Sulfate spring|12=draft}} == Onsen Town == The onsen town clusters around {{ill|Minakami Station|ja|水上駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Jōetsu Line|ja|上越線|12=draft}}, with hotels and inns densely packed along the steep banks of the upper {{ill|Tone River|ja|利根川|12=draft}}. The area is dotted with scenic spots such as {{ill|Suwa Gorge|ja|諏訪峡|12=draft}} and Minakami Gorge, attracting numerous visitors during the autumn foliage season. The slow-moving {{ill|Horse-drawn carriage|ja|トテ馬車|12=draft}} rides are a signature attraction of Minakami Onsen. Bridges over the Tone River, such as Yuhara Bridge and Minakami Bridge, are known for their nighttime illumination. Traditional amusements, including {{ill|Smart ball|ja|スマートボール|12=draft}} parlors, evoke nostalgia and draw modern visitors. Despite its focus on overnight stays, day-use facilities are limited but increasing. Many inns now accommodate day-use visitors. Public facilities include the town-run "Yuterume Tanigawa" and the "Fureai Koryukan," which offers foot baths. == History == According to legend, Minakami Onsen was founded during the {{ill|Eiroku era|ja|永禄年間|12=draft}} when the monk Kaiō discovered steam rising from a cliff along the Tone River. Before the opening of the {{ill|Shimizu Tunnel|ja|清水トンネル|12=draft}}, the area was a remote mountain retreat. Writers like {{ill|Bokusui Wakayama|ja|若山牧水|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osamu Dazai|ja|太宰治|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hakushū Kitahara|ja|北原白秋|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Akiko Yosano|ja|与謝野晶子|12=draft}} were captivated by the harmony of hot springs and dramatic valleys. The area was then known as Yuhara Onsen, a name still in occasional use. The opening of the {{ill|Jōetsu Line|ja|上越線|12=draft}} in 1931 brought Minakami Onsen closer to the {{ill|Greater Tokyo Area|ja|首都圏|12=draft}}. The 1966 {{ill|Kikufuji Hotel fire|ja|菊富士ホテル火災|12=draft}} marked a significant event in its history. The construction of the {{ill|Kan-Etsu Expressway|ja|関越自動車道|12=draft}} and the introduction of express trains like the {{ill|Yukemuri Express|ja|水上列車|12=draft}} led to its rise as a major recreation destination. However, the shift towards group tours and nightlife transformed the onsen into a bustling entertainment hub, complete with bars and show pubs. Following the {{ill|economic bubble collapse|ja|バブル崩壊|12=draft}}, declining group tours and changing tourist preferences forced the onsen to refocus. Today, it aims to leverage its natural surroundings, integrating {{ill|skiing|ja|スキー|12=draft}}, {{ill|rafting|ja|ラフティング|12=draft}}, and other outdoor activities to develop as a recreational resort. == Recent Issues == The {{ill|Ōshimizu Tunnel|ja|大清水トンネル|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Jōetsu Shinkansen|ja|上越新幹線|12=draft}} passes 50 meters beneath the hot spring's aquifers, causing decreased water flow. In 1979, the issue was addressed in the {{ill|House of Representatives of Japan|ja|衆議院|12=draft}}, prompting the {{ill|Japan Railway Construction Public Corporation|ja|日本鉄道建設公団|12=draft}} to drill new wells. The 2004 {{ill|onsen fraud scandal|ja|温泉偽装問題|1=Onsen Fraud Controversy|qq=Onsen Fraud Scandal|12=draft}} revealed that some facilities used tap water while advertising natural hot springs, raising concerns over misrepresentation and improper tax collection. The {{ill|abandoned Hotel Ōmiya|ja|ホテル大宮|12=draft}}, located near Minakami Station, remains an unresolved issue for the local community. == Access == * Train: {{ill|Jōetsu Line|ja|上越線|12=draft}}, Minakami Station, approximately 5 minutes on foot * Car: Exit at Minakami Interchange on the {{ill|Kan-Etsu Expressway|ja|関越自動車道|12=draft}}, via National Route 291 == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * ''Disappearing Ruins of Japan: Hidden History Through Ruins,'' Taiyo Publishing, 2014 == See Also == * {{ill|Ii Yudana|ja|いい湯だな|12=draft}} (song by {{ill|The Dukes Aces|ja|デューク・エイセス|12=draft}}) - Features Minakami in the fourth verse == External Links == * [https://www.enjoy-minakami.jp/ Minakami Perfect Guide] - Official tourism website of Minakami {{citation needed|Minakami Onsen}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Onsen in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Minakami]] [[Category:Minakami Onsen]] [[Category:Sulfate Spring]] [[Category:Copper-Iron Spring]] [[Category:Pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:水上温泉]] [[en:Minakami Onsen]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Minakami Onsen}} {{Onsen}}

The area is dotted with scenic spots such as {{ill|Suwa Gorge|ja|諏訪峡|12=draft|12=draft}} and Minakami Gorge, attracting numerous visitors during the autumn foliage season. The slow-moving {{ill|Horse-drawn carriage|ja|トテ馬車|12=draft|12=draft}} rides are a signature attraction of Minakami Onsen. Bridges over the Tone River, such as Yuhara Bridge and Minakami Bridge, are known for their nighttime illumination. Traditional amusements, including {{ill|Smart ball|ja|スマートボール|12=draft|12=draft}} parlors, evoke nostalgia and draw modern visitors. Despite its focus on overnight stays, day-use facilities are limited but increasing. Many inns now accommodate day-use visitors. Public facilities include the town-run "Yuterume Tanigawa" and the "Fureai Koryukan," which offers foot baths. == History == According to legend, Minakami Onsen was founded during the {{ill|Eiroku era|ja|永禄年間|12=draft|12=draft}} when the monk Kaiō discovered steam rising from a cliff along the Tone River. Before the opening of the {{ill|Shimizu Tunnel|ja|清水トンネル|12=draft|12=draft}}, the area was a remote mountain retreat. Writers like {{ill|Bokusui Wakayama|ja|若山牧水|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osamu Dazai|ja|太宰治|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hakushū Kitahara|ja|北原白秋|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Akiko Yosano|ja|与謝野晶子|12=draft|12=draft}} were captivated by the harmony of hot springs and dramatic valleys. The area was then known as Yuhara Onsen, a name still in occasional use. The opening of the {{ill|Jōetsu Line|ja|上越線|12=draft|12=draft}} in 1931 brought Minakami Onsen closer to the {{ill|Greater Tokyo Area|ja|首都圏|12=draft|12=draft}}. The 1966 {{ill|Kikufuji Hotel fire|ja|菊富士ホテル火災|12=draft|12=draft}} marked a significant event in its history. The construction of the {{ill|Kan-Etsu Expressway|ja|関越自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}} and the introduction of express trains like the {{ill|Yukemuri Express|ja|水上列車|12=draft|12=draft}} led to its rise as a major recreation destination. However, the shift towards group tours and nightlife transformed the onsen into a bustling entertainment hub, complete with bars and show pubs. Following the {{ill|economic bubble collapse|ja|バブル崩壊|12=draft|12=draft}}, declining group tours and changing tourist preferences forced the onsen to refocus. Today, it aims to leverage its natural surroundings, integrating {{ill|skiing|ja|スキー|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|rafting|ja|ラフティング|12=draft|12=draft}}, and other outdoor activities to develop as a recreational resort. == Recent Issues == The {{ill|Ōshimizu Tunnel|ja|大清水トンネル|12=draft|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Jōetsu Shinkansen|ja|上越新幹線|12=draft|12=draft}} passes 50 meters beneath the hot spring's aquifers, causing decreased water flow. In 1979, the issue was addressed in the {{ill|House of Representatives of Japan|ja|衆議院|12=draft|12=draft}}, prompting the {{ill|Japan Railway Construction Public Corporation|ja|日本鉄道建設公団|12=draft|12=draft}} to drill new wells. The 2004 {{ill|onsen fraud scandal|ja|温泉偽装問題|1=Onsen Fraud Controversy|qq=Onsen Fraud Scandal|12=draft|12=draft}} revealed that some facilities used tap water while advertising natural hot springs, raising concerns over misrepresentation and improper tax collection. The {{ill|abandoned Hotel Ōmiya|ja|ホテル大宮|12=draft|12=draft}}, located near Minakami Station, remains an unresolved issue for the local community. == Access == * Train: {{ill|Jōetsu Line|ja|上越線|12=draft|12=draft}}, Minakami Station, approximately 5 minutes on foot * Car: Exit at Minakami Interchange on the {{ill|Kan-Etsu Expressway|ja|関越自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}, via National Route 291 == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * ''Disappearing Ruins of Japan: Hidden History Through Ruins,'' Taiyo Publishing, 2014 == See Also == * {{ill|Ii Yudana|ja|いい湯だな|12=draft|12=draft}} (song by {{ill|The Dukes Aces|ja|デューク・エイセス|12=draft|12=draft}}) - Features Minakami in the fourth verse == External Links == * [https://www.enjoy-minakami.jp/ Minakami Perfect Guide] - Official tourism website of Minakami {{citation needed|Minakami Onsen}} {{draft categories| }} {{Onsen}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Onsen in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Minakami]] [[Category:Minakami Onsen]] [[Category:Sulfate Spring]] [[Category:Copper-Iron Spring]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Minakami Onsen]] [[ja:水上温泉]] [[en:Minakami Onsen]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Minakami Onsen}}

Car: Exit at {{ill|Minakami Interchange|ja|水上インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} on {{ill|Kan-Etsu Expressway|ja|関越自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}, follow National Route 291 |Hot spring type = {{ill|Sulfate spring|ja|硫酸塩泉|1=Sulfate spring|12=draft|12=draft}} |Flow rate = |pH = |Classification of fluid = |Classification of osmotic pressure = |Number of accommodations = |Total capacity = |Annual bathers = |Year of statistics = |External link = http://www.minakamionsen.com/ |Remarks = }} '''Minakami Onsen''' (水上温泉) is an onsen located in {{ill|Minakami|ja|みなかみ町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tone District, Gunma Prefecture. The area south of {{ill|Mount Tanigawa|ja|谷川岳|12=draft|12=draft}} is known as {{ill|Minakami Onsenkyo|ja|水上温泉郷|12=draft|12=draft}}, with Minakami Onsen serving as its central hub. Following {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦|12=draft|12=draft}}, its excellent {{ill|transportation access|ja|交通アクセス|12=draft|12=draft}} facilitated rapid expansion, establishing it as one of the major onsen areas in the prefecture alongside {{ill|Kusatsu Onsen|ja|草津温泉|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉|12=draft|12=draft}}. In the {{ill|Jomo Karuta|ja|上毛かるた|12=draft|12=draft}}, it is celebrated as "Mi no fuda: Minakami-Tanigawa, skiing and climbing." == Hot Spring Properties == * Calcium - {{ill|Sulfate spring|ja|硫酸塩泉|1=Sulfate spring|12=draft|12=draft}} == Onsen Town == The onsen town clusters around {{ill|Minakami Station|ja|水上駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Jōetsu Line|ja|上越線|12=draft|12=draft}}, with hotels and inns densely packed along the steep banks of the upper {{ill|Tone River|ja|利根川|12=draft|12=draft}}. The area is dotted with scenic spots such as {{ill|Suwa Gorge|ja|諏訪峡|12=draft|12=draft}} and Minakami Gorge, attracting numerous visitors during the autumn foliage season. The slow-moving {{ill|Horse-drawn carriage|ja|トテ馬車|12=draft|12=draft}} rides are a signature attraction of Minakami Onsen. Bridges over the Tone River, such as Yuhara Bridge and Minakami Bridge, are known for their nighttime illumination. Traditional amusements, including {{ill|Smart ball|ja|スマートボール|12=draft|12=draft}} parlors, evoke nostalgia and draw modern visitors. Despite its focus on overnight stays, day-use facilities are limited but increasing. Many inns now accommodate day-use visitors. Public facilities include the town-run "Yuterume Tanigawa" and the "Fureai Koryukan," which offers foot baths. == History == According to legend, Minakami Onsen was founded during the {{ill|Eiroku era|ja|永禄年間|12=draft|12=draft}} when the monk Kaiō discovered steam rising from a cliff along the Tone River. Before the opening of the {{ill|Shimizu Tunnel|ja|清水トンネル|12=draft|12=draft}}, the area was a remote mountain retreat. Writers like {{ill|Bokusui Wakayama|ja|若山牧水|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osamu Dazai|ja|太宰治|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hakushū Kitahara|ja|北原白秋|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Akiko Yosano|ja|与謝野晶子|12=draft|12=draft}} were captivated by the harmony of hot springs and dramatic valleys. The area was then known as Yuhara Onsen, a name still in occasional use. The opening of the {{ill|Jōetsu Line|ja|上越線|12=draft|12=draft}} in 1931 brought Minakami Onsen closer to the {{ill|Greater Tokyo Area|ja|首都圏|12=draft|12=draft}}. The 1966 {{ill|Kikufuji Hotel fire|ja|菊富士ホテル火災|12=draft|12=draft}} marked a significant event in its history. The construction of the {{ill|Kan-Etsu Expressway|ja|関越自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}} and the introduction of express trains like the {{ill|Yukemuri Express|ja|水上列車|12=draft|12=draft}} led to its rise as a major recreation destination. However, the shift towards group tours and nightlife transformed the onsen into a bustling entertainment hub, complete with bars and show pubs. Following the {{ill|economic bubble collapse|ja|バブル崩壊|12=draft|12=draft}}, declining group tours and changing tourist preferences forced the onsen to refocus. Today, it aims to leverage its natural surroundings, integrating {{ill|skiing|ja|スキー|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|rafting|ja|ラフティング|12=draft|12=draft}}, and other outdoor activities to develop as a recreational resort. == Recent Issues == The {{ill|Ōshimizu Tunnel|ja|大清水トンネル|12=draft|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Jōetsu Shinkansen|ja|上越新幹線|12=draft|12=draft}} passes 50 meters beneath the hot spring's aquifers, causing decreased water flow. In 1979, the issue was addressed in the {{ill|House of Representatives of Japan|ja|衆議院|12=draft|12=draft}}, prompting the {{ill|Japan Railway Construction Public Corporation|ja|日本鉄道建設公団|12=draft|12=draft}} to drill new wells. The 2004 {{ill|onsen fraud scandal|ja|温泉偽装問題|1=Onsen Fraud Controversy|qq=Onsen Fraud Scandal|12=draft|12=draft}} revealed that some facilities used tap water while advertising natural hot springs, raising concerns over misrepresentation and improper tax collection. The {{ill|abandoned Hotel Ōmiya|ja|ホテル大宮|12=draft|12=draft}}, located near Minakami Station, remains an unresolved issue for the local community. == Access == * Train: {{ill|Jōetsu Line|ja|上越線|12=draft|12=draft}}, Minakami Station, approximately 5 minutes on foot * Car: Exit at Minakami Interchange on the {{ill|Kan-Etsu Expressway|ja|関越自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}, via National Route 291 == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * ''Disappearing Ruins of Japan: Hidden History Through Ruins,'' Taiyo Publishing, 2014 == See Also == * {{ill|Ii Yudana|ja|いい湯だな|12=draft|12=draft}} (song by {{ill|The Dukes Aces|ja|デューク・エイセス|12=draft|12=draft}}) - Features Minakami in the fourth verse == External Links == * [https://www.enjoy-minakami.jp/ Minakami Perfect Guide] - Official tourism website of Minakami {{citation needed|Minakami Onsen}} {{draft categories| }} {{Onsen}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Onsen in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Minakami]] [[Category:Minakami Onsen]] [[Category:Sulfate Spring]] [[Category:Copper-Iron Spring]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Minakami Onsen]] [[ja:水上温泉]] [[en:Minakami Onsen]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Minakami Onsen}} [[Category:Ambox transclusions]]

In 1979, the issue was addressed in the {{ill|House of Representatives of Japan|ja|衆議院}}, prompting the {{ill|Japan Railway Construction Public Corporation|ja|日本鉄道建設公団}} to drill new wells. The 2004 {{ill|onsen fraud scandal|ja|温泉偽装問題|1=Onsen Fraud Controversy|qq=Onsen Fraud Scandal}} revealed that some facilities used tap water while advertising natural hot springs, raising concerns over misrepresentation and improper tax collection. The {{ill|abandoned Hotel Ōmiya|ja|ホテル大宮}}, located near Minakami Station, remains an unresolved issue for the local community. == Access == * Train: {{ill|Jōetsu Line|ja|上越線}}, Minakami Station, approximately 5 minutes on foot * Car: Exit at Minakami Interchange on the {{ill|Kan-Etsu Expressway|ja|関越自動車道}}, via National Route 291 == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * ''Disappearing Ruins of Japan: Hidden History Through Ruins,'' Taiyo Publishing, 2014 == See Also == * {{ill|Ii Yudana|ja|いい湯だな}} (song by {{ill|The Dukes Aces|ja|デューク・エイセス}}) - Features Minakami in the fourth verse == External Links == * [https://www.enjoy-minakami.jp/ Minakami Perfect Guide] - Official tourism website of Minakami {{citation needed|Minakami Onsen}} {{draft categories| }} {{Onsen}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Onsen in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Minakami]] [[Category:Minakami Onsen]] [[Category:Sulfate Spring]] [[Category:Copper-Iron Spring]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Minakami Onsen]] [[ja:水上温泉]] [[en:Minakami Onsen]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Minakami Onsen}} [[Category:Ambox transclusions]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Minakami Onsen]][[Category:qq]]

revision.comment: 39 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:水上温泉 up to rev 95165560
revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2017年3月}} {{日本の温泉地 |名称 = 水上温泉 |画像 = Minakami Onsen 01.jpg |コメント = [[利根川]]のほとり水上館 |所在地 = [[群馬県]][[利根郡]][[みなかみ町]] | 緯度度 = 36 | 緯度分 = 45 | 緯度秒 = 59 | 経度度 = 138 |経度分 = 57 | 経度秒 = 29 |交通アクセス = 鉄道：[[上越線]][[水上駅]]下車から徒歩約5分
車：[[関越自動車道]][[水上インターチェンジ|水上インターチェンジIC]]から[[国道291号]]経由 |泉質 = [[硫酸塩泉]] |湧出量 = |pH = |液性の分類 = |浸透圧の分類 = |宿泊施設数 = |総収容人員数 = |年間浴客数 = |統計年度 = |外部リンク = http://www.minakamionsen.com/ |特記事項 = }} '''水上温泉'''（みなかみおんせん）は、[[群馬県]][[利根郡]][[みなかみ町]]にある[[温泉]]。[[谷川岳]]南麓一帯を[[水上温泉郷]]と呼び、その根幹を成す中心温泉地である。[[第二次世界大戦]]後になって[[#アクセス|交通アクセスの良さ]]を理由に大幅に規模を拡大し、[[草津温泉]]や[[伊香保温泉]]と並ぶ県下有数の温泉地として発展した。「[[上毛かるた]]」では、「み」の札に「水上谷川　スキーと登山」として採録されている。 == 泉質 == * カルシウム - [[硫酸塩泉]] == 温泉街 == [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR東日本]][[上越線]]の[[水上駅]]近く、[[利根川]]上流の渓流沿いの崖に所狭しと大小の宿泊施設が櫛比する。その合間を縫って諏訪峡や水上峡があり、[[紅葉]]シーズンには多くの観光客で賑わう。温泉街をゆっくりと走るトテ[[馬車]]は水上温泉を代表する[[風物詩]]となっている。利根川には幾つもの橋が架かっており、湯原橋、水上橋は[[ライトアップ]]で知られる。[[スマートボール]]などの遊技場を有する昔ながらの[[温泉街]]は、今日では物珍しさによって注目を浴びている部分がある。 宿泊客を対象とした温泉地であるために、日帰りの温泉施設は規模に反して少ないが、今日では多くの旅館が日帰り客に対応するようになっている。また、町営の温泉施設「湯テルメ・谷川」や[[足湯]]が楽しめる「ふれあい交流館」などがあり、多様化するニーズに対応する姿勢を見せている。 == 歴史 == [[開湯伝説]]としては、[[永禄]]年間に海翁和尚が利根川の崖から立ち上る煙を発見したのが草創と伝えられる。[[清水トンネル]]が開通するまでは、一帯は隔絶された[[深山幽谷]]の温泉地として知られた。しかしながら、温泉情緒と雄大な渓谷美の自然が調和した温泉として多くの文人に愛され、牧水以外にも[[太宰治]]、[[北原白秋]]、[[与謝野晶子]]など多くの文人墨客が訪れたという。当時は水上温泉という名ではなく、湯原温泉と呼ばれた。今日でもこの地名を用いることがある。 [[1931年]]（[[昭和]]6年）に[[日本国有鉄道|国鉄]]上越線が開通すると[[首都圏 (日本)|首都圏]]の[[奥座敷]]として注目を浴びた。[[1966年]]（昭和41年）、[[菊富士ホテル火災]]が発生した。 以後は[[水上 (列車)|急行「ゆけむり」・特急「谷川」]]の運行、[[国道]]の整備や[[関越自動車道]]との接続などにより、団体旅行客を多数収容できる県下有数の行楽温泉地として発展した。平衡して歓楽的要素も強くなり、飲み屋やショーパブなども多く出店して、大規模な[[風俗街]]を形成した。 しかし、それにより[[熱海温泉]]や[[鬼怒川温泉]]などと同様に閉鎖的な温泉街を形成し、[[バブル崩壊|バブル経済崩壊]]後、団体旅行客の減少や旅行客のニーズの変化に伴って急激に衰退を余儀なくされた。今日では生き残りを賭けて、従来のイメージを払拭すべく、豊かな自然に着目し、一帯を[[レクリエーション]]拠点と位置付け、[[スキー]]や[[ラフティング]]などの[[アウトドア]]スポーツに力を入れ、[[レジャー]]一体型の温泉保養リゾートを目指しており、一定の成果が見られるようになっている。川沿いの遊歩道整備、地場食材を扱う農産物直売所とレストランを併設した「食彩の駅 M Forest」開業など活性化に取り組んでいる【知っ得News】地場産の希少な食材満喫 群馬・水上温泉『[[産経新聞]]』朝刊2022年7月15日13面。 ===近年の主な問題=== [[上越新幹線]][[清水トンネル|大清水トンネル]]が工事された際、泉脈の50m下をトンネルが通ったことにより、源泉枯れや湯量減少が発生した。[[1979年]]には[[衆議院]]の予算委員会でも問題として取り上げられ、[[日本鉄道建設公団]]より補償として新規源泉の[[ボーリング]]開発が行われた。 [[2004年]]に発生した[[温泉偽装問題]]では、温泉表記をしながら水道水を利用していた温泉が存在すると報じられた。また、一部旅館では温泉のみ徴収可能な[[入湯税]]も取っていたとして問題となった。 水上駅前に[[廃墟]]化した[[ホテル大宮]]があり、地元民は頭を悩ませているという『消えゆく日本の廃墟 廃墟が語る日本の裏歴史』68-69頁。。 == アクセス == * 鉄道：[[上越線]][[水上駅]]下車から徒歩約5分 * 車：[[関越自動車道]][[水上インターチェンジ]]から[[国道291号]]経由 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 『消えゆく日本の廃墟 廃墟が語る日本の裏歴史』[[大洋図書]]、2014年 == 関連項目 == * [[いい湯だな]]（[[デューク・エイセス]]）- 4番目の歌詞 == 外部リンク == * [https://www.enjoy-minakami.jp/ みなかみパーフェクトガイド] - みなかみ町観光協会 {{温泉}} {{デフォルトソート:みなかみおんせん}} [[Category:群馬県の温泉]] [[Category:みなかみ町の地理]]

{{Cite sources|date=March 2017}} {{Onsen in Japan |Name = Minakami Onsen |image = Minakami Onsen 01.jpg |Caption = {{ill|Tone River|ja|利根川}} riverside view at Minakami-kan |Location = {{ill|Minakami|ja|みなかみ町}}, Tone District, Gunma Prefecture | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 45 | Latitude seconds = 59 | Longitude degrees = 138 | Longitude minutes = 57 | Longitude seconds = 29 |Access = Train: {{ill|Jōetsu Line|ja|上越線}}, alight at Minakami Station, approximately 5 minutes on foot
Car: Exit at {{ill|Minakami Interchange|ja|水上インターチェンジ}} on {{ill|Kan-Etsu Expressway|ja|関越自動車道}}, follow National Route 291 |Hot spring type = {{ill|Sulfate spring|ja|硫酸塩泉|1=Sulfate spring}} |Flow rate = |pH = |Classification of fluid = |Classification of osmotic pressure = |Number of accommodations = |Total capacity = |Annual bathers = |Year of statistics = |External link = http://www.minakamionsen.com/ |Remarks = }} '''Minakami Onsen''' (水上温泉) is an onsen located in {{ill|Minakami|ja|みなかみ町}}, Tone District, Gunma Prefecture. The area south of {{ill|Mount Tanigawa|ja|谷川岳}} is known as {{ill|Minakami Onsenkyo|ja|水上温泉郷}}, with Minakami Onsen serving as its central hub. Following {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, its excellent {{ill|transportation access|ja|交通アクセス}} facilitated rapid expansion, establishing it as one of the major onsen areas in the prefecture alongside {{ill|Kusatsu Onsen|ja|草津温泉}} and {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉}}. In the {{ill|Jomo Karuta|ja|上毛かるた}}, it is celebrated as "Mi no fuda: Minakami-Tanigawa, skiing and climbing." == Hot Spring Properties == * Calcium - {{ill|Sulfate spring|ja|硫酸塩泉|1=Sulfate spring}} == Onsen Town == The onsen town clusters around {{ill|Minakami Station|ja|水上駅}} on the {{ill|Jōetsu Line|ja|上越線}}, with hotels and inns densely packed along the steep banks of the upper {{ill|Tone River|ja|利根川}}. The area is dotted with scenic spots such as {{ill|Suwa Gorge|ja|諏訪峡}} and Minakami Gorge, attracting numerous visitors during the autumn foliage season. The slow-moving {{ill|Horse-drawn carriage|ja|トテ馬車}} rides are a signature attraction of Minakami Onsen. Bridges over the Tone River, such as Yuhara Bridge and Minakami Bridge, are known for their nighttime illumination. Traditional amusements, including {{ill|Smart ball|ja|スマートボール}} parlors, evoke nostalgia and draw modern visitors. Despite its focus on overnight stays, day-use facilities are limited but increasing. Many inns now accommodate day-use visitors. Public facilities include the town-run "Yuterume Tanigawa" and the "Fureai Koryukan," which offers foot baths. == History == According to legend, Minakami Onsen was founded during the {{ill|Eiroku era|ja|永禄年間}} when the monk Kaiō discovered steam rising from a cliff along the Tone River. Before the opening of the {{ill|Shimizu Tunnel|ja|清水トンネル}}, the area was a remote mountain retreat. Writers like {{ill|Bokusui Wakayama|ja|若山牧水}}, {{ill|Osamu Dazai|ja|太宰治}}, {{ill|Hakushū Kitahara|ja|北原白秋}}, and {{ill|Akiko Yosano|ja|与謝野晶子}} were captivated by the harmony of hot springs and dramatic valleys. The area was then known as Yuhara Onsen, a name still in occasional use. The opening of the {{ill|Jōetsu Line|ja|上越線}} in 1931 brought Minakami Onsen closer to the {{ill|Greater Tokyo Area|ja|首都圏}}. The 1966 {{ill|Kikufuji Hotel fire|ja|菊富士ホテル火災}} marked a significant event in its history. The construction of the {{ill|Kan-Etsu Expressway|ja|関越自動車道}} and the introduction of express trains like the {{ill|Yukemuri Express|ja|水上列車}} led to its rise as a major recreation destination. However, the shift towards group tours and nightlife transformed the onsen into a bustling entertainment hub, complete with bars and show pubs. Following the {{ill|economic bubble collapse|ja|バブル崩壊}}, declining group tours and changing tourist preferences forced the onsen to refocus. Today, it aims to leverage its natural surroundings, integrating {{ill|skiing|ja|スキー}}, {{ill|rafting|ja|ラフティング}}, and other outdoor activities to develop as a recreational resort. == Recent Issues == The {{ill|Ōshimizu Tunnel|ja|大清水トンネル}} of the {{ill|Jōetsu Shinkansen|ja|上越新幹線}} passes 50 meters beneath the hot spring's aquifers, causing decreased water flow. In 1979, the issue was addressed in the {{ill|House of Representatives of Japan|ja|衆議院}}, prompting the {{ill|Japan Railway Construction Public Corporation|ja|日本鉄道建設公団}} to drill new wells. The 2004 {{ill|onsen fraud scandal|ja|温泉偽装問題|1=Onsen Fraud Controversy|qq=Onsen Fraud Scandal}} revealed that some facilities used tap water while advertising natural hot springs, raising concerns over misrepresentation and improper tax collection. The {{ill|abandoned Hotel Ōmiya|ja|ホテル大宮}}, located near Minakami Station, remains an unresolved issue for the local community. == Access == * Train: {{ill|Jōetsu Line|ja|上越線}}, Minakami Station, approximately 5 minutes on foot * Car: Exit at Minakami Interchange on the {{ill|Kan-Etsu Expressway|ja|関越自動車道}}, via National Route 291 == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * ''Disappearing Ruins of Japan: Hidden History Through Ruins,'' Taiyo Publishing, 2014 == See Also == * {{ill|Ii Yudana|ja|いい湯だな}} (song by {{ill|The Dukes Aces|ja|デューク・エイセス}}) - Features Minakami in the fourth verse == External Links == * [https://www.enjoy-minakami.jp/ Minakami Perfect Guide] - Official tourism website of Minakami {{citation needed|Minakami Onsen}} {{draft categories| }} {{Onsen}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Onsen in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Minakami]] [[Category:Minakami Onsen]] [[Category:Sulfate Spring]] [[Category:Copper-Iron Spring]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Minakami Onsen]] [[ja:水上温泉]] [[en:Minakami Onsen]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Minakami Onsen}} [[Category:Ambox transclusions]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Minakami Onsen]][[Category:qq]] {{translated page|ja|水上温泉|version=95165560|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{Cite sources|date=March 2017}} {{Onsen in Japan |Name = Minakami Onsen |image = Minakami Onsen 01.jpg |Caption = {{ill|Tone River|ja|利根川|12=draft}} riverside view at Minakami-kan |Location = {{ill|Minakami|ja|みなかみ町|12=draft}}, Tone District, Gunma Prefecture | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 45 | Latitude seconds = 59 | Longitude degrees = 138 | Longitude minutes = 57 | Longitude seconds = 29 |Access = Train: {{ill|Jōetsu Line|ja|上越線|12=draft}}, alight at Minakami Station, approximately 5 minutes on foot
Car: Exit at {{ill|Minakami Interchange|ja|水上インターチェンジ|12=draft}} on {{ill|Kan-Etsu Expressway|ja|関越自動車道|12=draft}}, follow National Route 291 |Hot spring type = {{ill|Sulfate spring|ja|硫酸塩泉|1=Sulfate spring|12=draft}} |Flow rate = |pH = |Classification of fluid = |Classification of osmotic pressure = |Number of accommodations = |Total capacity = |Annual bathers = |Year of statistics = |External link = http://www.minakamionsen.com/ |Remarks = }} '''Minakami Onsen''' (水上温泉) is an onsen located in {{ill|Minakami|ja|みなかみ町|12=draft}}, Tone District, Gunma Prefecture. The area south of {{ill|Mount Tanigawa|ja|谷川岳|12=draft}} is known as {{ill|Minakami Onsenkyo|ja|水上温泉郷|12=draft}}, with Minakami Onsen serving as its central hub. Following {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦|12=draft}}, its excellent {{ill|transportation access|ja|交通アクセス|12=draft}} facilitated rapid expansion, establishing it as one of the major onsen areas in the prefecture alongside {{ill|Kusatsu Onsen|ja|草津温泉|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉|12=draft}}. In the {{ill|Jomo Karuta|ja|上毛かるた|12=draft}}, it is celebrated as "Mi no fuda: Minakami-Tanigawa, skiing and climbing." == Hot Spring Properties == * Calcium - {{ill|Sulfate spring|ja|硫酸塩泉|1=Sulfate spring|12=draft}} == Onsen Town == The onsen town clusters around {{ill|Minakami Station|ja|水上駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Jōetsu Line|ja|上越線|12=draft}}, with hotels and inns densely packed along the steep banks of the upper {{ill|Tone River|ja|利根川|12=draft}}. The area is dotted with scenic spots such as {{ill|Suwa Gorge|ja|諏訪峡|12=draft}} and Minakami Gorge, attracting numerous visitors during the autumn foliage season. The slow-moving {{ill|Horse-drawn carriage|ja|トテ馬車|12=draft}} rides are a signature attraction of Minakami Onsen. Bridges over the Tone River, such as Yuhara Bridge and Minakami Bridge, are known for their nighttime illumination. Traditional amusements, including {{ill|Smart ball|ja|スマートボール|12=draft}} parlors, evoke nostalgia and draw modern visitors. Despite its focus on overnight stays, day-use facilities are limited but increasing. Many inns now accommodate day-use visitors. Public facilities include the town-run "Yuterume Tanigawa" and the "Fureai Koryukan," which offers foot baths. == History == According to legend, Minakami Onsen was founded during the {{ill|Eiroku era|ja|永禄年間|12=draft}} when the monk Kaiō discovered steam rising from a cliff along the Tone River. Before the opening of the {{ill|Shimizu Tunnel|ja|清水トンネル|12=draft}}, the area was a remote mountain retreat. Writers like {{ill|Bokusui Wakayama|ja|若山牧水|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osamu Dazai|ja|太宰治|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hakushū Kitahara|ja|北原白秋|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Akiko Yosano|ja|与謝野晶子|12=draft}} were captivated by the harmony of hot springs and dramatic valleys. The area was then known as Yuhara Onsen, a name still in occasional use. The opening of the {{ill|Jōetsu Line|ja|上越線|12=draft}} in 1931 brought Minakami Onsen closer to the {{ill|Greater Tokyo Area|ja|首都圏|12=draft}}. The 1966 {{ill|Kikufuji Hotel fire|ja|菊富士ホテル火災|12=draft}} marked a significant event in its history. The construction of the {{ill|Kan-Etsu Expressway|ja|関越自動車道|12=draft}} and the introduction of express trains like the {{ill|Yukemuri Express|ja|水上列車|12=draft}} led to its rise as a major recreation destination. However, the shift towards group tours and nightlife transformed the onsen into a bustling entertainment hub, complete with bars and show pubs. Following the {{ill|economic bubble collapse|ja|バブル崩壊|12=draft}}, declining group tours and changing tourist preferences forced the onsen to refocus. Today, it aims to leverage its natural surroundings, integrating {{ill|skiing|ja|スキー|12=draft}}, {{ill|rafting|ja|ラフティング|12=draft}}, and other outdoor activities to develop as a recreational resort. == Recent Issues == The {{ill|Ōshimizu Tunnel|ja|大清水トンネル|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Jōetsu Shinkansen|ja|上越新幹線|12=draft}} passes 50 meters beneath the hot spring's aquifers, causing decreased water flow. In 1979, the issue was addressed in the {{ill|House of Representatives of Japan|ja|衆議院|12=draft}}, prompting the {{ill|Japan Railway Construction Public Corporation|ja|日本鉄道建設公団|12=draft}} to drill new wells. The 2004 {{ill|onsen fraud scandal|ja|温泉偽装問題|1=Onsen Fraud Controversy|qq=Onsen Fraud Scandal|12=draft}} revealed that some facilities used tap water while advertising natural hot springs, raising concerns over misrepresentation and improper tax collection. The {{ill|abandoned Hotel Ōmiya|ja|ホテル大宮|12=draft}}, located near Minakami Station, remains an unresolved issue for the local community. == Access == * Train: {{ill|Jōetsu Line|ja|上越線|12=draft}}, Minakami Station, approximately 5 minutes on foot * Car: Exit at Minakami Interchange on the {{ill|Kan-Etsu Expressway|ja|関越自動車道|12=draft}}, via National Route 291 == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * ''Disappearing Ruins of Japan: Hidden History Through Ruins,'' Taiyo Publishing, 2014 == See Also == * {{ill|Ii Yudana|ja|いい湯だな|12=draft}} (song by {{ill|The Dukes Aces|ja|デューク・エイセス|12=draft}}) - Features Minakami in the fourth verse == External Links == * [https://www.enjoy-minakami.jp/ Minakami Perfect Guide] - Official tourism website of Minakami {{citation needed|Minakami Onsen}} {{draft categories| }} {{Onsen}} {{draft categories| }} {{translated page|ja|水上温泉|version=95165560|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Onsen in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Minakami]] [[Category:Minakami Onsen]] [[Category:Sulfate Spring]] [[Category:Copper-Iron Spring]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Ambox transclusions]] }} [[sn:Minakami Onsen]] [[ja:水上温泉]] [[en:Minakami Onsen]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Minakami Onsen}}

However, the shift towards group tours and nightlife transformed the onsen into a bustling entertainment hub, complete with bars and show pubs. Following the {{ill|economic bubble collapse|ja|バブル崩壊|qq=draft}}, declining group tours and changing tourist preferences forced the onsen to refocus. Today, it aims to leverage its natural surroundings, integrating {{ill|skiing|ja|スキー|qq=draft}}, {{ill|rafting|ja|ラフティング|qq=draft}}, and other outdoor activities to develop as a recreational resort. == Recent Issues == The {{ill|Ōshimizu Tunnel|ja|大清水トンネル|qq=draft}} of the {{ill|Jōetsu Shinkansen|ja|上越新幹線|qq=draft}} passes 50 meters beneath the hot spring's aquifers, causing decreased water flow. In 1979, the issue was addressed in the {{ill|House of Representatives of Japan|ja|衆議院|qq=draft}}, prompting the {{ill|Japan Railway Construction Public Corporation|ja|日本鉄道建設公団|qq=draft}} to drill new wells. The 2004 {{ill|onsen fraud scandal|ja|温泉偽装問題|1=Onsen Fraud Controversy|qq=Onsen Fraud Scandal|qq=draft}} revealed that some facilities used tap water while advertising natural hot springs, raising concerns over misrepresentation and improper tax collection. The {{ill|abandoned Hotel Ōmiya|ja|ホテル大宮|qq=draft}}, located near Minakami Station, remains an unresolved issue for the local community. == Access == * Train: {{ill|Jōetsu Line|ja|上越線|qq=draft}}, Minakami Station, approximately 5 minutes on foot * Car: Exit at Minakami Interchange on the {{ill|Kan-Etsu Expressway|ja|関越自動車道|qq=draft}}, via National Route 291 == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * ''Disappearing Ruins of Japan: Hidden History Through Ruins,'' Taiyo Publishing, 2014 == See Also == * {{ill|Ii Yudana|ja|いい湯だな|qq=draft}} (song by {{ill|The Dukes Aces|ja|デューク・エイセス|qq=draft}}) - Features Minakami in the fourth verse == External Links == * [https://www.enjoy-minakami.jp/ Minakami Perfect Guide] - Official tourism website of Minakami {{citation needed|Minakami Onsen}} {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|水上温泉|version=95165560|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Minakami Onsen}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Onsen in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Minakami]] [[Category:Minakami Onsen]] [[Category:Sulfate Spring]] [[Category:Copper-Iron Spring]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Minakami Onsen]] [[ja:水上温泉]] [[en:Minakami Onsen]]

{{Cite sources|date=March 2017}} {{Onsen in Japan |Name = Minakami Onsen |image = Minakami Onsen 01.jpg |Caption = {{ill|Tone River|ja|利根川|qq=draft}} riverside view at Minakami-kan |Location = {{ill|Minakami|ja|みなかみ町|qq=draft}}, Tone District, Gunma Prefecture | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 45 | Latitude seconds = 59 | Longitude degrees = 138 | Longitude minutes = 57 | Longitude seconds = 29 |Access = Train: {{ill|Jōetsu Line|ja|上越線|qq=draft}}, alight at Minakami Station, approximately 5 minutes on foot
Car: Exit at {{ill|Minakami Interchange|ja|水上インターチェンジ|qq=draft}} on {{ill|Kan-Etsu Expressway|ja|関越自動車道|qq=draft}}, follow National Route 291 |Hot spring type = {{ill|Sulfate spring|ja|硫酸塩泉|1=Sulfate spring|qq=draft}} |Flow rate = |pH = |Classification of fluid = |Classification of osmotic pressure = |Number of accommodations = |Total capacity = |Annual bathers = |Year of statistics = |External link = http://www.minakamionsen.com/ |Remarks = }} '''Minakami Onsen''' (水上温泉) is an onsen located in {{ill|Minakami|ja|みなかみ町|qq=draft}}, Tone District, Gunma Prefecture. The area south of {{ill|Mount Tanigawa|ja|谷川岳|qq=draft}} is known as {{ill|Minakami Onsenkyo|ja|水上温泉郷|qq=draft}}, with Minakami Onsen serving as its central hub. Following {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦|qq=draft}}, its excellent {{ill|transportation access|ja|交通アクセス|qq=draft}} facilitated rapid expansion, establishing it as one of the major onsen areas in the prefecture alongside {{ill|Kusatsu Onsen|ja|草津温泉|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉|qq=draft}}. In the {{ill|Jomo Karuta|ja|上毛かるた|qq=draft}}, it is celebrated as "Mi no fuda: Minakami-Tanigawa, skiing and climbing." == Hot Spring Properties == * Calcium - {{ill|Sulfate spring|ja|硫酸塩泉|1=Sulfate spring|qq=draft}} == Onsen Town == The onsen town clusters around {{ill|Minakami Station|ja|水上駅|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Jōetsu Line|ja|上越線|qq=draft}}, with hotels and inns densely packed along the steep banks of the upper {{ill|Tone River|ja|利根川|qq=draft}}. The area is dotted with scenic spots such as {{ill|Suwa Gorge|ja|諏訪峡|qq=draft}} and Minakami Gorge, attracting numerous visitors during the autumn foliage season. The slow-moving {{ill|Horse-drawn carriage|ja|トテ馬車|qq=draft}} rides are a signature attraction of Minakami Onsen. Bridges over the Tone River, such as Yuhara Bridge and Minakami Bridge, are known for their nighttime illumination. Traditional amusements, including {{ill|Smart ball|ja|スマートボール|qq=draft}} parlors, evoke nostalgia and draw modern visitors. Despite its focus on overnight stays, day-use facilities are limited but increasing. Many inns now accommodate day-use visitors. Public facilities include the town-run "Yuterume Tanigawa" and the "Fureai Koryukan," which offers foot baths. == History == According to legend, Minakami Onsen was founded during the {{ill|Eiroku era|ja|永禄年間|qq=draft}} when the monk Kaiō discovered steam rising from a cliff along the Tone River. Before the opening of the {{ill|Shimizu Tunnel|ja|清水トンネル|qq=draft}}, the area was a remote mountain retreat. Writers like {{ill|Bokusui Wakayama|ja|若山牧水|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Osamu Dazai|ja|太宰治|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hakushū Kitahara|ja|北原白秋|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Akiko Yosano|ja|与謝野晶子|qq=draft}} were captivated by the harmony of hot springs and dramatic valleys. The area was then known as Yuhara Onsen, a name still in occasional use. The opening of the {{ill|Jōetsu Line|ja|上越線|qq=draft}} in 1931 brought Minakami Onsen closer to the {{ill|Greater Tokyo Area|ja|首都圏|qq=draft}}. The 1966 {{ill|Kikufuji Hotel fire|ja|菊富士ホテル火災|qq=draft}} marked a significant event in its history. The construction of the {{ill|Kan-Etsu Expressway|ja|関越自動車道|qq=draft}} and the introduction of express trains like the {{ill|Yukemuri Express|ja|水上列車|qq=draft}} led to its rise as a major recreation destination. However, the shift towards group tours and nightlife transformed the onsen into a bustling entertainment hub, complete with bars and show pubs. Following the {{ill|economic bubble collapse|ja|バブル崩壊|qq=draft}}, declining group tours and changing tourist preferences forced the onsen to refocus. Today, it aims to leverage its natural surroundings, integrating {{ill|skiing|ja|スキー|qq=draft}}, {{ill|rafting|ja|ラフティング|qq=draft}}, and other outdoor activities to develop as a recreational resort. == Recent Issues == The {{ill|Ōshimizu Tunnel|ja|大清水トンネル|qq=draft}} of the {{ill|Jōetsu Shinkansen|ja|上越新幹線|qq=draft}} passes 50 meters beneath the hot spring's aquifers, causing decreased water flow. In 1979, the issue was addressed in the {{ill|House of Representatives of Japan|ja|衆議院|qq=draft}}, prompting the {{ill|Japan Railway Construction Public Corporation|ja|日本鉄道建設公団|qq=draft}} to drill new wells. The 2004 {{ill|onsen fraud scandal|ja|温泉偽装問題|1=Onsen Fraud Controversy|qq=Onsen Fraud Scandal|qq=draft}} revealed that some facilities used tap water while advertising natural hot springs, raising concerns over misrepresentation and improper tax collection. The {{ill|abandoned Hotel Ōmiya|ja|ホテル大宮|qq=draft}}, located near Minakami Station, remains an unresolved issue for the local community. == Access == * Train: {{ill|Jōetsu Line|ja|上越線|qq=draft}}, Minakami Station, approximately 5 minutes on foot * Car: Exit at Minakami Interchange on the {{ill|Kan-Etsu Expressway|ja|関越自動車道|qq=draft}}, via National Route 291 == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * ''Disappearing Ruins of Japan: Hidden History Through Ruins,'' Taiyo Publishing, 2014 == See Also == * {{ill|Ii Yudana|ja|いい湯だな|qq=draft}} (song by {{ill|The Dukes Aces|ja|デューク・エイセス|qq=draft}}) - Features Minakami in the fourth verse == External Links == * [https://www.enjoy-minakami.jp/ Minakami Perfect Guide] - Official tourism website of Minakami {{citation needed|Minakami Onsen}} {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|水上温泉|version=95165560|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Onsen in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Minakami]] [[Category:Minakami Onsen]] [[Category:Sulfate Spring]] [[Category:Copper-Iron Spring]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Minakami Onsen]] [[ja:水上温泉]] [[en:Minakami Onsen]]
