[[co:commons:Category:Minakami Onsen]][[ja:水上温泉]] [[co:commons:Category:Minakami Onsen]][[ja:水上温泉]] [[sn:commons:Category:Minakami Onsen]][[ja:水上温泉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11548228]] Wikidata name: Minakami Onsen [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=March 2017}} {{Onsen in Japan |Name = Minakami Onsen |image = Minakami Onsen 01.jpg |Caption = {{ill|Tone River|en|Tone River|WD=Q177452|lt=Tone River}} riverside view at Minakami-kan |Location = {{ill|Minakami, Gunma|en|Minakami, Gunma|WD=Q1345518|lt=Minakami}}, Tone District, Gunma Prefecture | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 45 | Latitude seconds = 59 | Longitude degrees = 138 | Longitude minutes = 57 | Longitude seconds = 29 |Access = Train: {{ill|Jōetsu Line|en|Jōetsu Line|WD=Q1135061|lt=Jōetsu Line}}, alight at Minakami Station, approximately 5 minutes on foot
Car: Exit at {{ill|Minakami Interchange|ja|水上インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minakami Interchange|1=Minakami interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11129404}} on {{ill|Kan-etsu Expressway|en|Kan-etsu Expressway|WD=Q1059893|lt=Kan-Etsu Expressway}}, follow National Route 291 |Hot spring type = {{ill|Sulfate spring|ja|硫酸塩泉|1=Sulfate spring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sulfate spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Sulfate spring|qq=}} |Flow rate = |pH = |Classification of fluid = |Classification of osmotic pressure = |Number of accommodations = |Total capacity = |Annual bathers = |Year of statistics = |External link = http://www.minakamionsen.com/ |Remarks = }} '''Minakami Onsen''' (水上温泉) is an onsen located in {{ill|Minakami, Gunma|en|Minakami, Gunma|WD=Q1345518|lt=Minakami}}, Tone District, Gunma Prefecture. The area south of {{ill|Mount Tanigawa|en|Mount Tanigawa|WD=Q1109030|lt=Mount Tanigawa}} is known as {{ill|Minakami Onsenkyo|ja|水上温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minakami Onsenkyo|1=Minakami Onsenkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11548229}}, with Minakami Onsen serving as its central hub. Following {{ill|World War II|en|World War II|WD=Q362|lt=World War II}}, its excellent {{ill|transportation access|ja|交通アクセス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/transportation access|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アクセス|1=Access|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q338902}} facilitated rapid expansion, establishing it as one of the major onsen areas in the prefecture alongside {{ill|Kusatsu Onsen|en|Kusatsu Onsen|WD=Q3137477|lt=Kusatsu Onsen}} and {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Onsen|qq=}}. In the {{ill|Jomo Karuta|ja|上毛かるた|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jomo Karuta|1=Jōmō Karuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17216027}}, it is celebrated as "Mi no fuda: Minakami-Tanigawa, skiing and climbing." == Hot Spring Properties == * Calcium - {{ill|Sulfate spring|ja|硫酸塩泉|1=Sulfate spring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sulfate spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Sulfate spring|qq=}} == Onsen Town == The onsen town clusters around {{ill|Minakami Station|en|Minakami Station|WD=Q1770451|lt=Minakami Station}} on the {{ill|Jōetsu Line|en|Jōetsu Line|WD=Q1135061|lt=Jōetsu Line}}, with hotels and inns densely packed along the steep banks of the upper {{ill|Tone River|en|Tone River|WD=Q177452|lt=Tone River}}. The area is dotted with scenic spots such as {{ill|Suwa Gorge|ja|諏訪峡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Gorge|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and Minakami Gorge, attracting numerous visitors during the autumn foliage season. The slow-moving {{ill|Horse-drawn carriage|ja|トテ馬車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horse-drawn carriage|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 馬車|1=horse-drawn vehicle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1112950}} rides are a signature attraction of Minakami Onsen. Bridges over the Tone River, such as Yuhara Bridge and Minakami Bridge, are known for their nighttime illumination. Traditional amusements, including {{ill|Smart ball|ja|スマートボール|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Smart ball|1=Smart ball|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11313289}} parlors, evoke nostalgia and draw modern visitors. Despite its focus on overnight stays, day-use facilities are limited but increasing. Many inns now accommodate day-use visitors. Public facilities include the town-run "Yuterume Tanigawa" and the "Fureai Koryukan," which offers foot baths. == History == According to legend, Minakami Onsen was founded during the {{ill|Eiroku era|ja|永禄年間|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiroku era|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} when the monk Kaiō discovered steam rising from a cliff along the Tone River. Before the opening of the {{ill|Shimizu Tunnel|ja|清水トンネル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimizu Tunnel|1=Shimizu Tunnels|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q75220055}}, the area was a remote mountain retreat. Writers like {{ill|Bokusui Wakayama|en|Bokusui Wakayama|WD=Q532557|lt=Bokusui Wakayama}}, {{ill|Osamu Dazai|en|Osamu Dazai|WD=Q317685|lt=Osamu Dazai}}, {{ill|Hakushū Kitahara|en|Hakushū Kitahara|WD=Q1066026|lt=Hakushū Kitahara}}, and {{ill|Yosano Akiko|en|Yosano Akiko|WD=Q464104|lt=Akiko Yosano}} were captivated by the harmony of hot springs and dramatic valleys. The area was then known as Yuhara Onsen, a name still in occasional use. The opening of the {{ill|Jōetsu Line|en|Jōetsu Line|WD=Q1135061|lt=Jōetsu Line}} in 1931 brought Minakami Onsen closer to the {{ill|Capital region|en|Capital region|WD=Q1062177|lt=Greater Tokyo Area}}. The 1966 {{ill|Kikufuji Hotel fire|ja|菊富士ホテル火災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikufuji Hotel fire|1=Kikufuji Hotel fire|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619620}} marked a significant event in its history. The construction of the {{ill|Kan-etsu Expressway|en|Kan-etsu Expressway|WD=Q1059893|lt=Kan-Etsu Expressway}} and the introduction of express trains like the {{ill|Yukemuri Express|ja|水上列車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukemuri Express|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} led to its rise as a major recreation destination. However, the shift towards group tours and nightlife transformed the onsen into a bustling entertainment hub, complete with bars and show pubs. Following the {{ill|Lost Decades|en|Lost Decades|WD=Q1141122|lt=economic bubble collapse}}, declining group tours and changing tourist preferences forced the onsen to refocus. Today, it aims to leverage its natural surroundings, integrating {{ill|Skiing|en|Skiing|WD=Q130949|lt=skiing}}, {{ill|Rafting|en|Rafting|WD=Q207238|lt=rafting}}, and other outdoor activities to develop as a recreational resort. == Recent Issues == The {{ill|Ōshimizu Tunnel|ja|大清水トンネル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōshimizu Tunnel|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 清水トンネル|1=Shimizu Tunnels|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q75220055}} of the {{ill|Jōetsu Shinkansen|en|Jōetsu Shinkansen|WD=Q912219|lt=Jōetsu Shinkansen}} passes 50 meters beneath the hot spring's aquifers, causing decreased water flow. In 1979, the issue was addressed in the {{ill|House of Representatives (Japan)|en|House of Representatives (Japan)|WD=Q659587|lt=House of Representatives of Japan}}, prompting the {{ill|Japan Railway Construction Public Corporation|en|Japan Railway Construction Public Corporation|WD=Q6157806|lt=Japan Railway Construction Public Corporation}} to drill new wells. The 2004 {{ill|onsen fraud scandal|ja|温泉偽装問題|1=Onsen Fraud Controversy|qq=Onsen Fraud Scandal|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/onsen fraud scandal|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Fraud Controversy|qq=}} revealed that some facilities used tap water while advertising natural hot springs, raising concerns over misrepresentation and improper tax collection. The {{ill|abandoned Hotel Ōmiya|ja|ホテル大宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/abandoned Hotel Ōmiya|1=Abandoned Hotel Ōmiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85873736}}, located near Minakami Station, remains an unresolved issue for the local community. == Access == * Train: {{ill|Jōetsu Line|en|Jōetsu Line|WD=Q1135061|lt=Jōetsu Line}}, Minakami Station, approximately 5 minutes on foot * Car: Exit at Minakami Interchange on the {{ill|Kan-etsu Expressway|en|Kan-etsu Expressway|WD=Q1059893|lt=Kan-Etsu Expressway}}, via National Route 291 == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * ''Disappearing Ruins of Japan: Hidden History Through Ruins,'' Taiyo Publishing, 2014 == See Also == * {{ill|Ii Yudana|ja|いい湯だな|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Yudana|1=Ii Yudana|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11259008}} (song by {{ill|The Dukes Aces|ja|デューク・エイセス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Dukes Aces|1=Duke Aces|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11320788}}) - Features Minakami in the fourth verse == External Links == * [https://www.enjoy-minakami.jp/ Minakami Perfect Guide] - Official tourism website of Minakami {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|水上温泉|version=95165560|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:水上温泉]] {{Commons category|Minakami Onsen}} {{wikidata link|Q11548228}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Copper-Iron Spring]] [[Category:Hot springs of Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Sulfate spring]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

In the {{ill|Jomo Karuta|ja|上毛かるた|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jomo Karuta|1=Jōmō Karuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17216027}}, it is celebrated as "Mi no fuda: Minakami-Tanigawa, skiing and climbing." == Hot Spring Properties == * Calcium - {{ill|Sulfate spring|ja|硫酸塩泉|1=Sulfate spring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sulfate spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Sulfate spring|qq=}} == Onsen Town == The onsen town clusters around {{ill|Minakami Station|en|Minakami Station|WD=Q1770451|lt=Minakami Station}} on the {{ill|Jōetsu Line|en|Jōetsu Line|WD=Q1135061|lt=Jōetsu Line}}, with hotels and inns densely packed along the steep banks of the upper {{ill|Tone River|en|Tone River|WD=Q177452|lt=Tone River}}. Writers like {{ill|Bokusui Wakayama|en|Bokusui Wakayama|WD=Q532557|lt=Bokusui Wakayama}}, {{ill|Osamu Dazai|en|Osamu Dazai|WD=Q317685|lt=Osamu Dazai}}, {{ill|Hakushū Kitahara|en|Hakushū Kitahara|WD=Q1066026|lt=Hakushū Kitahara}}, and {{ill|Yosano Akiko|en|Yosano Akiko|WD=Q464104|lt=Akiko Yosano}} were captivated by the harmony of hot springs and dramatic valleys. The area was then known as Yuhara Onsen, a name still in occasional use. The opening of the {{ill|Jōetsu Line|en|Jōetsu Line|WD=Q1135061|lt=Jōetsu Line}} in 1931 brought Minakami Onsen closer to the {{ill|Capital region|en|Capital region|WD=Q1062177|lt=Greater Tokyo Area}}. The 1966 {{ill|Kikufuji Hotel fire|ja|菊富士ホテル火災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikufuji Hotel fire|1=Kikufuji Hotel fire|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619620}} marked a significant event in its history. The construction of the {{ill|Kan-etsu Expressway|en|Kan-etsu Expressway|WD=Q1059893|lt=Kan-Etsu Expressway}} and the introduction of express trains like the {{ill|Yukemuri Express|ja|水上列車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukemuri Express|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} led to its rise as a major recreation destination. However, the shift towards group tours and nightlife transformed the onsen into a bustling entertainment hub, complete with bars and show pubs. Following the {{ill|Lost Decades|en|Lost Decades|WD=Q1141122|lt=economic bubble collapse}}, declining group tours and changing tourist preferences forced the onsen to refocus. Today, it aims to leverage its natural surroundings, integrating {{ill|Skiing|en|Skiing|WD=Q130949|lt=skiing}}, {{ill|Rafting|en|Rafting|WD=Q207238|lt=rafting}}, and other outdoor activities to develop as a recreational resort. == Recent Issues == The {{ill|Ōshimizu Tunnel|ja|大清水トンネル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōshimizu Tunnel|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 清水トンネル|1=Shimizu Tunnels|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q75220055}} of the {{ill|Jōetsu Shinkansen|en|Jōetsu Shinkansen|WD=Q912219|lt=Jōetsu Shinkansen}} passes 50 meters beneath the hot spring's aquifers, causing decreased water flow. In 1979, the issue was addressed in the {{ill|House of Representatives (Japan)|en|House of Representatives (Japan)|WD=Q659587|lt=House of Representatives of Japan}}, prompting the {{ill|Japan Railway Construction Public Corporation|en|Japan Railway Construction Public Corporation|WD=Q6157806|lt=Japan Railway Construction Public Corporation}} to drill new wells. The 2004 {{ill|onsen fraud scandal|ja|温泉偽装問題|1=Onsen Fraud Controversy|qq=Onsen Fraud Scandal|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/onsen fraud scandal|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Fraud Controversy|qq=}} revealed that some facilities used tap water while advertising natural hot springs, raising concerns over misrepresentation and improper tax collection. The {{ill|abandoned Hotel Ōmiya|ja|ホテル大宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/abandoned Hotel Ōmiya|1=Abandoned Hotel Ōmiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85873736}}, located near Minakami Station, remains an unresolved issue for the local community. == Access == * Train: {{ill|Jōetsu Line|en|Jōetsu Line|WD=Q1135061|lt=Jōetsu Line}}, Minakami Station, approximately 5 minutes on foot * Car: Exit at Minakami Interchange on the {{ill|Kan-etsu Expressway|en|Kan-etsu Expressway|WD=Q1059893|lt=Kan-Etsu Expressway}}, via National Route 291 == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * ''Disappearing Ruins of Japan: Hidden History Through Ruins,'' Taiyo Publishing, 2014 == See Also == * {{ill|Ii Yudana|ja|いい湯だな|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Yudana|1=Ii Yudana|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11259008}} (song by {{ill|The Dukes Aces|ja|デューク・エイセス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Dukes Aces|1=Duke Aces|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11320788}}) - Features Minakami in the fourth verse == External Links == * [https://www.enjoy-minakami.jp/ Minakami Perfect Guide] - Official tourism website of Minakami {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|水上温泉|version=95165560|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:水上温泉]] {{Commons category|Minakami Onsen}} {{wikidata link|Q11548228}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Copper-Iron Spring]] [[Category:Hot springs of Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Sulfate spring]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Minakami Onsen]][[ja:水上温泉]] [[co:commons:Category:Minakami Onsen]][[ja:水上温泉]] [[sn:commons:Category:Minakami Onsen]][[ja:水上温泉]] {{Unreferenced|date=March 2017}} {{Onsen in Japan |Name = Minakami Onsen |image = Minakami Onsen 01.jpg |Caption = {{ill|Tone River|en|Tone River|WD=Q177452|lt=Tone River}} riverside view at Minakami-kan |Location = {{ill|Minakami, Gunma|en|Minakami, Gunma|WD=Q1345518|lt=Minakami}}, Tone District, Gunma Prefecture | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 45 | Latitude seconds = 59 | Longitude degrees = 138 | Longitude minutes = 57 | Longitude seconds = 29 |Access = Train: {{ill|Jōetsu Line|en|Jōetsu Line|WD=Q1135061|lt=Jōetsu Line}}, alight at Minakami Station, approximately 5 minutes on foot
Car: Exit at {{ill|Minakami Interchange|ja|水上インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minakami Interchange|1=Minakami interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11129404}} on {{ill|Kan-etsu Expressway|en|Kan-etsu Expressway|WD=Q1059893|lt=Kan-Etsu Expressway}}, follow National Route 291 |Hot spring type = {{ill|Sulfate spring|ja|硫酸塩泉|1=Sulfate spring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sulfate spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Sulfate spring|qq=}} |Flow rate = |pH = |Classification of fluid = |Classification of osmotic pressure = |Number of accommodations = |Total capacity = |Annual bathers = |Year of statistics = |External link = http://www.minakamionsen.com/ |Remarks = }} '''Minakami Onsen''' (水上温泉) is an onsen located in {{ill|Minakami, Gunma|en|Minakami, Gunma|WD=Q1345518|lt=Minakami}}, Tone District, Gunma Prefecture. The area south of {{ill|Mount Tanigawa|en|Mount Tanigawa|WD=Q1109030|lt=Mount Tanigawa}} is known as {{ill|Minakami Onsenkyo|ja|水上温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minakami Onsenkyo|1=Minakami Onsenkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11548229}}, with Minakami Onsen serving as its central hub. Following {{ill|World War II|en|World War II|WD=Q362|lt=World War II}}, its excellent {{ill|transportation access|ja|交通アクセス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/transportation access|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アクセス|1=Access|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q338902}} facilitated rapid expansion, establishing it as one of the major onsen areas in the prefecture alongside {{ill|Kusatsu Onsen|en|Kusatsu Onsen|WD=Q3137477|lt=Kusatsu Onsen}} and {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Onsen|qq=}}. In the {{ill|Jomo Karuta|ja|上毛かるた|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jomo Karuta|1=Jōmō Karuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17216027}}, it is celebrated as "Mi no fuda: Minakami-Tanigawa, skiing and climbing." == Hot Spring Properties == * Calcium - {{ill|Sulfate spring|ja|硫酸塩泉|1=Sulfate spring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sulfate spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Sulfate spring|qq=}} == Onsen Town == The onsen town clusters around {{ill|Minakami Station|en|Minakami Station|WD=Q1770451|lt=Minakami Station}} on the {{ill|Jōetsu Line|en|Jōetsu Line|WD=Q1135061|lt=Jōetsu Line}}, with hotels and inns densely packed along the steep banks of the upper {{ill|Tone River|en|Tone River|WD=Q177452|lt=Tone River}}. The area is dotted with scenic spots such as {{ill|Suwa Gorge|ja|諏訪峡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Gorge|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and Minakami Gorge, attracting numerous visitors during the autumn foliage season. The slow-moving {{ill|Horse-drawn carriage|ja|トテ馬車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horse-drawn carriage|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 馬車|1=horse-drawn vehicle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1112950}} rides are a signature attraction of Minakami Onsen. Bridges over the Tone River, such as Yuhara Bridge and Minakami Bridge, are known for their nighttime illumination. Traditional amusements, including {{ill|Smart ball|ja|スマートボール|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Smart ball|1=Smart ball|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11313289}} parlors, evoke nostalgia and draw modern visitors. Despite its focus on overnight stays, day-use facilities are limited but increasing. Many inns now accommodate day-use visitors. Public facilities include the town-run "Yuterume Tanigawa" and the "Fureai Koryukan," which offers foot baths. == History == According to legend, Minakami Onsen was founded during the {{ill|Eiroku era|ja|永禄年間|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiroku era|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} when the monk Kaiō discovered steam rising from a cliff along the Tone River. Before the opening of the {{ill|Shimizu Tunnel|ja|清水トンネル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimizu Tunnel|1=Shimizu Tunnels|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q75220055}}, the area was a remote mountain retreat. Writers like {{ill|Bokusui Wakayama|en|Bokusui Wakayama|WD=Q532557|lt=Bokusui Wakayama}}, {{ill|Osamu Dazai|en|Osamu Dazai|WD=Q317685|lt=Osamu Dazai}}, {{ill|Hakushū Kitahara|en|Hakushū Kitahara|WD=Q1066026|lt=Hakushū Kitahara}}, and {{ill|Yosano Akiko|en|Yosano Akiko|WD=Q464104|lt=Akiko Yosano}} were captivated by the harmony of hot springs and dramatic valleys. The area was then known as Yuhara Onsen, a name still in occasional use. The opening of the {{ill|Jōetsu Line|en|Jōetsu Line|WD=Q1135061|lt=Jōetsu Line}} in 1931 brought Minakami Onsen closer to the {{ill|Capital region|en|Capital region|WD=Q1062177|lt=Greater Tokyo Area}}. The 1966 {{ill|Kikufuji Hotel fire|ja|菊富士ホテル火災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikufuji Hotel fire|1=Kikufuji Hotel fire|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619620}} marked a significant event in its history. The construction of the {{ill|Kan-etsu Expressway|en|Kan-etsu Expressway|WD=Q1059893|lt=Kan-Etsu Expressway}} and the introduction of express trains like the {{ill|Yukemuri Express|ja|水上列車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukemuri Express|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} led to its rise as a major recreation destination. However, the shift towards group tours and nightlife transformed the onsen into a bustling entertainment hub, complete with bars and show pubs. Following the {{ill|Lost Decades|en|Lost Decades|WD=Q1141122|lt=economic bubble collapse}}, declining group tours and changing tourist preferences forced the onsen to refocus. Today, it aims to leverage its natural surroundings, integrating {{ill|Skiing|en|Skiing|WD=Q130949|lt=skiing}}, {{ill|Rafting|en|Rafting|WD=Q207238|lt=rafting}}, and other outdoor activities to develop as a recreational resort. == Recent Issues == The {{ill|Ōshimizu Tunnel|ja|大清水トンネル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōshimizu Tunnel|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 清水トンネル|1=Shimizu Tunnels|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q75220055}} of the {{ill|Jōetsu Shinkansen|en|Jōetsu Shinkansen|WD=Q912219|lt=Jōetsu Shinkansen}} passes 50 meters beneath the hot spring's aquifers, causing decreased water flow. In 1979, the issue was addressed in the {{ill|House of Representatives (Japan)|en|House of Representatives (Japan)|WD=Q659587|lt=House of Representatives of Japan}}, prompting the {{ill|Japan Railway Construction Public Corporation|en|Japan Railway Construction Public Corporation|WD=Q6157806|lt=Japan Railway Construction Public Corporation}} to drill new wells. The 2004 {{ill|onsen fraud scandal|ja|温泉偽装問題|1=Onsen Fraud Controversy|qq=Onsen Fraud Scandal|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/onsen fraud scandal|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Fraud Controversy|qq=}} revealed that some facilities used tap water while advertising natural hot springs, raising concerns over misrepresentation and improper tax collection. The {{ill|abandoned Hotel Ōmiya|ja|ホテル大宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/abandoned Hotel Ōmiya|1=Abandoned Hotel Ōmiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85873736}}, located near Minakami Station, remains an unresolved issue for the local community. == Access == * Train: {{ill|Jōetsu Line|en|Jōetsu Line|WD=Q1135061|lt=Jōetsu Line}}, Minakami Station, approximately 5 minutes on foot * Car: Exit at Minakami Interchange on the {{ill|Kan-etsu Expressway|en|Kan-etsu Expressway|WD=Q1059893|lt=Kan-Etsu Expressway}}, via National Route 291 == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * ''Disappearing Ruins of Japan: Hidden History Through Ruins,'' Taiyo Publishing, 2014 == See Also == * {{ill|Ii Yudana|ja|いい湯だな|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Yudana|1=Ii Yudana|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11259008}} (song by {{ill|The Dukes Aces|ja|デューク・エイセス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Dukes Aces|1=Duke Aces|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11320788}}) - Features Minakami in the fourth verse == External Links == * [https://www.enjoy-minakami.jp/ Minakami Perfect Guide] - Official tourism website of Minakami {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|水上温泉|version=95165560|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:水上温泉]] {{Commons category|Minakami Onsen}} {{wikidata link|Q11548228}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Copper-Iron Spring]] [[Category:Hot springs of Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Sulfate spring]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Minakami Onsen]][[ja:水上温泉]] [[co:commons:Category:Minakami Onsen]][[ja:水上温泉]] [[sn:commons:Category:Minakami Onsen]][[ja:水上温泉]] {{Unreferenced|date=March 2017}} {{Onsen in Japan |Name = Minakami Onsen |image = Minakami Onsen 01.jpg |Caption = {{ill|Tone River|en|Tone River|WD=Q177452|lt=Tone River|1=Tone River}} riverside view at Minakami-kan |Location = {{ill|Minakami, Gunma|en|Minakami, Gunma|WD=Q1345518|lt=Minakami|1=Minakami, Gunma}}, Tone District, Gunma Prefecture | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 45 | Latitude seconds = 59 | Longitude degrees = 138 | Longitude minutes = 57 | Longitude seconds = 29 |Access = Train: {{ill|Jōetsu Line|en|Jōetsu Line|WD=Q1135061|lt=Jōetsu Line|1=Jōetsu Line}}, alight at Minakami Station, approximately 5 minutes on foot
Car: Exit at {{ill|Minakami Interchange|ja|水上インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minakami Interchange|1=Minakami interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11129404}} on {{ill|Kan-etsu Expressway|en|Kan-etsu Expressway|WD=Q1059893|lt=Kan-Etsu Expressway|1=Kan-etsu Expressway}}, follow National Route 291 |Hot spring type = {{ill|Sulfate spring|ja|硫酸塩泉|1=Sulfate spring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sulfate spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Sulfate spring|qq=}} |Flow rate = |pH = |Classification of fluid = |Classification of osmotic pressure = |Number of accommodations = |Total capacity = |Annual bathers = |Year of statistics = |External link = http://www.minakamionsen.com/ |Remarks = }} '''Minakami Onsen''' (水上温泉) is an onsen located in {{ill|Minakami, Gunma|en|Minakami, Gunma|WD=Q1345518|lt=Minakami|1=Minakami, Gunma}}, Tone District, Gunma Prefecture. The area south of {{ill|Mount Tanigawa|en|Mount Tanigawa|WD=Q1109030|lt=Mount Tanigawa|1=Mount Tanigawa}} is known as {{ill|Minakami Onsenkyo|ja|水上温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minakami Onsenkyo|1=Minakami Onsenkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11548229}}, with Minakami Onsen serving as its central hub. Following {{ill|World War II|en|World War II|WD=Q362|lt=World War II|1=World War II}}, its excellent {{ill|transportation access|ja|交通アクセス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/transportation access|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アクセス|1=Access|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q338902}} facilitated rapid expansion, establishing it as one of the major onsen areas in the prefecture alongside {{ill|Kusatsu Onsen|en|Kusatsu Onsen|WD=Q3137477|lt=Kusatsu Onsen|1=Kusatsu Onsen}} and {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Onsen|qq=}}. In the {{ill|Jomo Karuta|ja|上毛かるた|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jomo Karuta|1=Jōmō Karuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17216027}}, it is celebrated as "Mi no fuda: Minakami-Tanigawa, skiing and climbing." == Hot Spring Properties == * Calcium - {{ill|Sulfate spring|ja|硫酸塩泉|1=Sulfate spring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sulfate spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Sulfate spring|qq=}} == Onsen Town == The onsen town clusters around {{ill|Minakami Station|en|Minakami Station|WD=Q1770451|lt=Minakami Station|1=Minakami Station}} on the {{ill|Jōetsu Line|en|Jōetsu Line|WD=Q1135061|lt=Jōetsu Line|1=Jōetsu Line}}, with hotels and inns densely packed along the steep banks of the upper {{ill|Tone River|en|Tone River|WD=Q177452|lt=Tone River|1=Tone River}}. The area is dotted with scenic spots such as {{ill|Suwa Gorge|ja|諏訪峡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Gorge|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and Minakami Gorge, attracting numerous visitors during the autumn foliage season. The slow-moving {{ill|Horse-drawn carriage|ja|トテ馬車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horse-drawn carriage|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 馬車|1=Horse-drawn vehicle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1112950}} rides are a signature attraction of Minakami Onsen. Bridges over the Tone River, such as Yuhara Bridge and Minakami Bridge, are known for their nighttime illumination. Traditional amusements, including {{ill|Smart ball|ja|スマートボール|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Smart ball|1=Smart ball|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11313289}} parlors, evoke nostalgia and draw modern visitors. Despite its focus on overnight stays, day-use facilities are limited but increasing. Many inns now accommodate day-use visitors. Public facilities include the town-run "Yuterume Tanigawa" and the "Fureai Koryukan," which offers foot baths. == History == According to legend, Minakami Onsen was founded during the {{ill|Eiroku era|ja|永禄年間|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiroku era|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} when the monk Kaiō discovered steam rising from a cliff along the Tone River. Before the opening of the {{ill|Shimizu Tunnel|ja|清水トンネル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimizu Tunnel|1=Shimizu Tunnels|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q75220055}}, the area was a remote mountain retreat. Writers like {{ill|Bokusui Wakayama|en|Bokusui Wakayama|WD=Q532557|lt=Bokusui Wakayama|1=Bokusui Wakayama}}, {{ill|Osamu Dazai|en|Osamu Dazai|WD=Q317685|lt=Osamu Dazai|1=Osamu Dazai}}, {{ill|Hakushū Kitahara|en|Hakushū Kitahara|WD=Q1066026|lt=Hakushū Kitahara|1=Hakushū Kitahara}}, and {{ill|Yosano Akiko|en|Yosano Akiko|WD=Q464104|lt=Akiko Yosano|1=Yosano Akiko}} were captivated by the harmony of hot springs and dramatic valleys. The area was then known as Yuhara Onsen, a name still in occasional use. The opening of the {{ill|Jōetsu Line|en|Jōetsu Line|WD=Q1135061|lt=Jōetsu Line|1=Jōetsu Line}} in 1931 brought Minakami Onsen closer to the {{ill|Capital region|en|Capital region|WD=Q1062177|lt=Greater Tokyo Area|1=Capital region}}. The 1966 {{ill|Kikufuji Hotel fire|ja|菊富士ホテル火災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikufuji Hotel fire|1=Q11619620|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619620}} marked a significant event in its history. The construction of the {{ill|Kan-etsu Expressway|en|Kan-etsu Expressway|WD=Q1059893|lt=Kan-Etsu Expressway|1=Kan-etsu Expressway}} and the introduction of express trains like the {{ill|Yukemuri Express|ja|水上列車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukemuri Express|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} led to its rise as a major recreation destination. However, the shift towards group tours and nightlife transformed the onsen into a bustling entertainment hub, complete with bars and show pubs. Following the {{ill|Lost Decades|en|Lost Decades|WD=Q1141122|lt=economic bubble collapse|1=Lost Decades}}, declining group tours and changing tourist preferences forced the onsen to refocus. Today, it aims to leverage its natural surroundings, integrating {{ill|Skiing|en|Skiing|WD=Q130949|lt=skiing|1=Skiing}}, {{ill|Rafting|en|Rafting|WD=Q207238|lt=rafting|1=Rafting}}, and other outdoor activities to develop as a recreational resort. == Recent Issues == The {{ill|Ōshimizu Tunnel|ja|大清水トンネル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōshimizu Tunnel|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 清水トンネル|1=Shimizu Tunnels|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q75220055}} of the {{ill|Jōetsu Shinkansen|en|Jōetsu Shinkansen|WD=Q912219|lt=Jōetsu Shinkansen|1=Jōetsu Shinkansen}} passes 50 meters beneath the hot spring's aquifers, causing decreased water flow. In 1979, the issue was addressed in the {{ill|House of Representatives (Japan)|en|House of Representatives (Japan)|WD=Q659587|lt=House of Representatives of Japan|1=House of Representatives (Japan)}}, prompting the {{ill|Japan Railway Construction Public Corporation|en|Japan Railway Construction Public Corporation|WD=Q6157806|lt=Japan Railway Construction Public Corporation|1=Japan Railway Construction Public Corporation}} to drill new wells. The 2004 {{ill|onsen fraud scandal|ja|温泉偽装問題|1=Onsen Fraud Controversy|qq=Onsen Fraud Scandal|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/onsen fraud scandal|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Fraud Controversy|qq=}} revealed that some facilities used tap water while advertising natural hot springs, raising concerns over misrepresentation and improper tax collection. The {{ill|abandoned Hotel Ōmiya|ja|ホテル大宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/abandoned Hotel Ōmiya|1=Abandoned Hotel Ōmiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85873736}}, located near Minakami Station, remains an unresolved issue for the local community. == Access == * Train: {{ill|Jōetsu Line|en|Jōetsu Line|WD=Q1135061|lt=Jōetsu Line|1=Jōetsu Line}}, Minakami Station, approximately 5 minutes on foot * Car: Exit at Minakami Interchange on the {{ill|Kan-etsu Expressway|en|Kan-etsu Expressway|WD=Q1059893|lt=Kan-Etsu Expressway|1=Kan-etsu Expressway}}, via National Route 291 == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * ''Disappearing Ruins of Japan: Hidden History Through Ruins,'' Taiyo Publishing, 2014 == See Also == * {{ill|Ii Yudana|ja|いい湯だな|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Yudana|1=Q11259008|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11259008}} (song by {{ill|The Dukes Aces|ja|デューク・エイセス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Dukes Aces|1=Duke Aces|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11320788}}) - Features Minakami in the fourth verse == External Links == * [https://www.enjoy-minakami.jp/ Minakami Perfect Guide] - Official tourism website of Minakami {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|水上温泉|version=95165560|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:水上温泉]] {{Commons category|Minakami Onsen}} {{wikidata link|Q11548228}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Copper-Iron Spring]] [[Category:Hot springs of Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Sulfate spring]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

Traditional amusements, including {{ill|Smart ball|ja|スマートボール|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Smart ball|1=Smart ball|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11313289}} parlors, evoke nostalgia and draw modern visitors. Despite its focus on overnight stays, day-use facilities are limited but increasing. Many inns now accommodate day-use visitors. Public facilities include the town-run "Yuterume Tanigawa" and the "Fureai Koryukan," which offers foot baths. == History == According to legend, Minakami Onsen was founded during the {{ill|Eiroku era|ja|永禄年間|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiroku era|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} when the monk Kaiō discovered steam rising from a cliff along the Tone River. Before the opening of the {{ill|Shimizu Tunnel|ja|清水トンネル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimizu Tunnel|1=Shimizu Tunnels|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q75220055}}, the area was a remote mountain retreat. Writers like {{ill|Bokusui Wakayama|en|Bokusui Wakayama|WD=Q532557|lt=Bokusui Wakayama|1=Bokusui Wakayama}}, {{ill|Osamu Dazai|en|Osamu Dazai|WD=Q317685|lt=Osamu Dazai|1=Osamu Dazai}}, {{ill|Hakushū Kitahara|en|Hakushū Kitahara|WD=Q1066026|lt=Hakushū Kitahara|1=Hakushū Kitahara}}, and {{ill|Yosano Akiko|en|Yosano Akiko|WD=Q464104|lt=Akiko Yosano|1=Yosano Akiko}} were captivated by the harmony of hot springs and dramatic valleys. The area was then known as Yuhara Onsen, a name still in occasional use. The opening of the {{ill|Jōetsu Line|en|Jōetsu Line|WD=Q1135061|lt=Jōetsu Line|1=Jōetsu Line}} in 1931 brought Minakami Onsen closer to the {{ill|Capital region|en|Capital region|WD=Q1062177|lt=Greater Tokyo Area|1=Capital region}}. The 1966 {{ill|Kikufuji Hotel fire|ja|菊富士ホテル火災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikufuji Hotel fire|1=Q11619620|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619620}} marked a significant event in its history. The construction of the {{ill|Kan-etsu Expressway|en|Kan-etsu Expressway|WD=Q1059893|lt=Kan-Etsu Expressway|1=Kan-etsu Expressway}} and the introduction of express trains like the {{ill|Yukemuri Express|ja|水上列車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukemuri Express|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} led to its rise as a major recreation destination. However, the shift towards group tours and nightlife transformed the onsen into a bustling entertainment hub, complete with bars and show pubs. Following the {{ill|Lost Decades|en|Lost Decades|WD=Q1141122|lt=economic bubble collapse|1=Lost Decades}}, declining group tours and changing tourist preferences forced the onsen to refocus. Today, it aims to leverage its natural surroundings, integrating {{ill|Skiing|en|Skiing|WD=Q130949|lt=skiing|1=Skiing}}, {{ill|Rafting|en|Rafting|WD=Q207238|lt=rafting|1=Rafting}}, and other outdoor activities to develop as a recreational resort. == Recent Issues == The {{ill|Ōshimizu Tunnel|ja|大清水トンネル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōshimizu Tunnel|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 清水トンネル|1=Shimizu Tunnels|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q75220055}} of the {{ill|Jōetsu Shinkansen|en|Jōetsu Shinkansen|WD=Q912219|lt=Jōetsu Shinkansen|1=Jōetsu Shinkansen}} passes 50 meters beneath the hot spring's aquifers, causing decreased water flow. In 1979, the issue was addressed in the {{ill|House of Representatives (Japan)|en|House of Representatives (Japan)|WD=Q659587|lt=House of Representatives of Japan|1=House of Representatives (Japan)}}, prompting the {{ill|Japan Railway Construction Public Corporation|en|Japan Railway Construction Public Corporation|WD=Q6157806|lt=Japan Railway Construction Public Corporation|1=Japan Railway Construction Public Corporation}} to drill new wells. The 2004 {{ill|onsen fraud scandal|ja|温泉偽装問題|1=Onsen Fraud Controversy|qq=Onsen Fraud Scandal|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/onsen fraud scandal|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Fraud Controversy|qq=}} revealed that some facilities used tap water while advertising natural hot springs, raising concerns over misrepresentation and improper tax collection. The {{ill|abandoned Hotel Ōmiya|ja|ホテル大宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/abandoned Hotel Ōmiya|1=Abandoned Hotel Ōmiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85873736}}, located near Minakami Station, remains an unresolved issue for the local community. == Access == * Train: {{ill|Jōetsu Line|en|Jōetsu Line|WD=Q1135061|lt=Jōetsu Line|1=Jōetsu Line}}, Minakami Station, approximately 5 minutes on foot * Car: Exit at Minakami Interchange on the {{ill|Kan-etsu Expressway|en|Kan-etsu Expressway|WD=Q1059893|lt=Kan-Etsu Expressway|1=Kan-etsu Expressway}}, via National Route 291 == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * ''Disappearing Ruins of Japan: Hidden History Through Ruins,'' Taiyo Publishing, 2014 == See Also == * {{ill|Ii Yudana|ja|いい湯だな|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Yudana|1=Q11259008|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11259008}} (song by {{ill|The Dukes Aces|ja|デューク・エイセス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Dukes Aces|1=Duke Aces|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11320788}}) - Features Minakami in the fourth verse == External Links == * [https://www.enjoy-minakami.jp/ Minakami Perfect Guide] - Official tourism website of Minakami {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|水上温泉|version=95165560|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:水上温泉]] {{Commons category|Minakami Onsen}} {{wikidata link|Q11548228}} [[Category:Copper-Iron Spring]] [[Category:Hot springs of Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Sulfate spring]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Minakami Onsen]][[ja:水上温泉]] [[co:commons:Category:Minakami Onsen]][[ja:水上温泉]] [[Category:sn_crud|commons:Category:Minakami Onsen]][[ja:水上温泉]] {{Unreferenced|date=March 2017}} {{Onsen in Japan |Name = Minakami Onsen |image = Minakami Onsen 01.jpg |Caption = {{ill|Tone River|en|Tone River|WD=Q177452|lt=Tone River|1=Tone River}} riverside view at Minakami-kan |Location = {{ill|Minakami, Gunma|en|Minakami, Gunma|WD=Q1345518|lt=Minakami|1=Minakami, Gunma}}, Tone District, Gunma Prefecture | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 45 | Latitude seconds = 59 | Longitude degrees = 138 | Longitude minutes = 57 | Longitude seconds = 29 |Access = Train: {{ill|Jōetsu Line|en|Jōetsu Line|WD=Q1135061|lt=Jōetsu Line|1=Jōetsu Line}}, alight at Minakami Station, approximately 5 minutes on foot
Car: Exit at {{ill|Minakami Interchange|ja|水上インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minakami Interchange|1=Minakami interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11129404}} on {{ill|Kan-etsu Expressway|en|Kan-etsu Expressway|WD=Q1059893|lt=Kan-Etsu Expressway|1=Kan-etsu Expressway}}, follow National Route 291 |Hot spring type = {{ill|Sulfate spring|ja|硫酸塩泉|1=Sulfate spring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sulfate spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Sulfate spring|qq=}} |Flow rate = |pH = |Classification of fluid = |Classification of osmotic pressure = |Number of accommodations = |Total capacity = |Annual bathers = |Year of statistics = |External link = http://www.minakamionsen.com/ |Remarks = }} '''Minakami Onsen''' (水上温泉) is an onsen located in {{ill|Minakami, Gunma|en|Minakami, Gunma|WD=Q1345518|lt=Minakami|1=Minakami, Gunma}}, Tone District, Gunma Prefecture. The area south of {{ill|Mount Tanigawa|en|Mount Tanigawa|WD=Q1109030|lt=Mount Tanigawa|1=Mount Tanigawa}} is known as {{ill|Minakami Onsenkyo|ja|水上温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minakami Onsenkyo|1=Minakami Onsenkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11548229}}, with Minakami Onsen serving as its central hub. Following {{ill|World War II|en|World War II|WD=Q362|lt=World War II|1=World War II}}, its excellent {{ill|transportation access|ja|交通アクセス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/transportation access|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アクセス|1=Access|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q338902}} facilitated rapid expansion, establishing it as one of the major onsen areas in the prefecture alongside {{ill|Kusatsu Onsen|en|Kusatsu Onsen|WD=Q3137477|lt=Kusatsu Onsen|1=Kusatsu Onsen}} and {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Onsen|qq=}}. In the {{ill|Jomo Karuta|ja|上毛かるた|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jomo Karuta|1=Jōmō Karuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17216027}}, it is celebrated as "Mi no fuda: Minakami-Tanigawa, skiing and climbing." == Hot Spring Properties == * Calcium - {{ill|Sulfate spring|ja|硫酸塩泉|1=Sulfate spring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sulfate spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Sulfate spring|qq=}} == Onsen Town == The onsen town clusters around {{ill|Minakami Station|en|Minakami Station|WD=Q1770451|lt=Minakami Station|1=Minakami Station}} on the {{ill|Jōetsu Line|en|Jōetsu Line|WD=Q1135061|lt=Jōetsu Line|1=Jōetsu Line}}, with hotels and inns densely packed along the steep banks of the upper {{ill|Tone River|en|Tone River|WD=Q177452|lt=Tone River|1=Tone River}}. The area is dotted with scenic spots such as {{ill|Suwa Gorge|ja|諏訪峡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Gorge|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and Minakami Gorge, attracting numerous visitors during the autumn foliage season. The slow-moving {{ill|Horse-drawn carriage|ja|トテ馬車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horse-drawn carriage|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 馬車|1=Horse-drawn vehicle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1112950}} rides are a signature attraction of Minakami Onsen. Bridges over the Tone River, such as Yuhara Bridge and Minakami Bridge, are known for their nighttime illumination. Traditional amusements, including {{ill|Smart ball|ja|スマートボール|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Smart ball|1=Smart ball|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11313289}} parlors, evoke nostalgia and draw modern visitors. Despite its focus on overnight stays, day-use facilities are limited but increasing. Many inns now accommodate day-use visitors. Public facilities include the town-run "Yuterume Tanigawa" and the "Fureai Koryukan," which offers foot baths. == History == According to legend, Minakami Onsen was founded during the {{ill|Eiroku era|ja|永禄年間|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiroku era|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} when the monk Kaiō discovered steam rising from a cliff along the Tone River. In 1979, the issue was addressed in the {{ill|House of Representatives (Japan)|en|House of Representatives (Japan)|WD=Q659587|lt=House of Representatives of Japan|1=House of Representatives (Japan)}}, prompting the {{ill|Japan Railway Construction Public Corporation|en|Japan Railway Construction Public Corporation|WD=Q6157806|lt=Japan Railway Construction Public Corporation|1=Japan Railway Construction Public Corporation}} to drill new wells. The 2004 {{ill|onsen fraud scandal|ja|温泉偽装問題|1=Onsen Fraud Controversy|qq=Onsen Fraud Scandal|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/onsen fraud scandal|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Fraud Controversy|qq=}} revealed that some facilities used tap water while advertising natural hot springs, raising concerns over misrepresentation and improper tax collection. The {{ill|abandoned Hotel Ōmiya|ja|ホテル大宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/abandoned Hotel Ōmiya|1=Abandoned Hotel Ōmiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85873736}}, located near Minakami Station, remains an unresolved issue for the local community. == Access == * Train: {{ill|Jōetsu Line|en|Jōetsu Line|WD=Q1135061|lt=Jōetsu Line|1=Jōetsu Line}}, Minakami Station, approximately 5 minutes on foot * Car: Exit at Minakami Interchange on the {{ill|Kan-etsu Expressway|en|Kan-etsu Expressway|WD=Q1059893|lt=Kan-Etsu Expressway|1=Kan-etsu Expressway}}, via National Route 291 == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * ''Disappearing Ruins of Japan: Hidden History Through Ruins,'' Taiyo Publishing, 2014 == See Also == * {{ill|Ii Yudana|ja|いい湯だな|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Yudana|1=Q11259008|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11259008}} (song by {{ill|The Dukes Aces|ja|デューク・エイセス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Dukes Aces|1=Duke Aces|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11320788}}) - Features Minakami in the fourth verse == External Links == * [https://www.enjoy-minakami.jp/ Minakami Perfect Guide] - Official tourism website of Minakami {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|水上温泉|version=95165560|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:水上温泉]] {{Commons category|Minakami Onsen}} {{wikidata link|Q11548228}} [[Category:Copper-Iron Spring]] [[Category:Hot springs of Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Sulfate spring]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Minakami Onsen]][[ja:水上温泉]] [[co:commons:Category:Minakami Onsen]][[ja:水上温泉]] [[ja:水上温泉]] {{Unreferenced|date=March 2017}} {{Onsen in Japan |Name = Minakami Onsen |image = Minakami Onsen 01.jpg |Caption = {{ill|Tone River|en|Tone River|qid=Q177452|lt=Tone River|1=Tone River}} riverside view at Minakami-kan |Location = {{ill|Minakami, Gunma|en|Minakami, Gunma|qid=Q1345518|lt=Minakami|1=Minakami, Gunma}}, Tone District, Gunma Prefecture | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 45 | Latitude seconds = 59 | Longitude degrees = 138 | Longitude minutes = 57 | Longitude seconds = 29 |Access = Train: {{ill|Jōetsu Line|en|Jōetsu Line|qid=Q1135061|lt=Jōetsu Line|1=Jōetsu Line}}, alight at Minakami Station, approximately 5 minutes on foot
Car: Exit at {{ill|Minakami Interchange|ja|水上インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minakami Interchange|1=Minakami interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11129404}} on {{ill|Kan-etsu Expressway|en|Kan-etsu Expressway|qid=Q1059893|lt=Kan-Etsu Expressway|1=Kan-etsu Expressway}}, follow National Route 291 |Hot spring type = {{ill|Sulfate spring|ja|硫酸塩泉|1=Sulfate spring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sulfate spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Sulfate spring|qq=}} |Flow rate = |pH = |Classification of fluid = |Classification of osmotic pressure = |Number of accommodations = |Total capacity = |Annual bathers = |Year of statistics = |External link = http://www.minakamionsen.com/ |Remarks = }} '''Minakami Onsen''' (水上温泉) is an onsen located in {{ill|Minakami, Gunma|en|Minakami, Gunma|qid=Q1345518|lt=Minakami|1=Minakami, Gunma}}, Tone District, Gunma Prefecture. The area south of {{ill|Mount Tanigawa|en|Mount Tanigawa|qid=Q1109030|lt=Mount Tanigawa|1=Mount Tanigawa}} is known as {{ill|Minakami Onsenkyo|ja|水上温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minakami Onsenkyo|1=Minakami Onsenkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11548229}}, with Minakami Onsen serving as its central hub. Following {{ill|World War II|en|World War II|qid=Q362|lt=World War II|1=World War II}}, its excellent {{ill|transportation access|ja|交通アクセス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/transportation access|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アクセス|1=Access|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q338902}} facilitated rapid expansion, establishing it as one of the major onsen areas in the prefecture alongside {{ill|Kusatsu Onsen|en|Kusatsu Onsen|qid=Q3137477|lt=Kusatsu Onsen|1=Kusatsu Onsen}} and {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Onsen|qq=}}. In the {{ill|Jomo Karuta|ja|上毛かるた|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jomo Karuta|1=Jōmō Karuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17216027}}, it is celebrated as "Mi no fuda: Minakami-Tanigawa, skiing and climbing." == Hot Spring Properties == * Calcium - {{ill|Sulfate spring|ja|硫酸塩泉|1=Sulfate spring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sulfate spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Sulfate spring|qq=}} == Onsen Town == The onsen town clusters around {{ill|Minakami Station|en|Minakami Station|qid=Q1770451|lt=Minakami Station|1=Minakami Station}} on the {{ill|Jōetsu Line|en|Jōetsu Line|qid=Q1135061|lt=Jōetsu Line|1=Jōetsu Line}}, with hotels and inns densely packed along the steep banks of the upper {{ill|Tone River|en|Tone River|qid=Q177452|lt=Tone River|1=Tone River}}. The area is dotted with scenic spots such as {{ill|Suwa Gorge|ja|諏訪峡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Gorge|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and Minakami Gorge, attracting numerous visitors during the autumn foliage season. The slow-moving {{ill|Horse-drawn carriage|ja|トテ馬車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horse-drawn carriage|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 馬車|1=Horse-drawn vehicle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1112950}} rides are a signature attraction of Minakami Onsen. Bridges over the Tone River, such as Yuhara Bridge and Minakami Bridge, are known for their nighttime illumination. Traditional amusements, including {{ill|Smart ball|ja|スマートボール|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Smart ball|1=Smart ball|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11313289}} parlors, evoke nostalgia and draw modern visitors. Despite its focus on overnight stays, day-use facilities are limited but increasing. Many inns now accommodate day-use visitors. Public facilities include the town-run "Yuterume Tanigawa" and the "Fureai Koryukan," which offers foot baths. == History == According to legend, Minakami Onsen was founded during the {{ill|Eiroku era|ja|永禄年間|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiroku era|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} when the monk Kaiō discovered steam rising from a cliff along the Tone River. Before the opening of the {{ill|Shimizu Tunnel|ja|清水トンネル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimizu Tunnel|1=Shimizu Tunnels|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q75220055}}, the area was a remote mountain retreat. Writers like {{ill|Bokusui Wakayama|en|Bokusui Wakayama|qid=Q532557|lt=Bokusui Wakayama|1=Bokusui Wakayama}}, {{ill|Osamu Dazai|en|Osamu Dazai|qid=Q317685|lt=Osamu Dazai|1=Osamu Dazai}}, {{ill|Hakushū Kitahara|en|Hakushū Kitahara|qid=Q1066026|lt=Hakushū Kitahara|1=Hakushū Kitahara}}, and {{ill|Yosano Akiko|en|Yosano Akiko|qid=Q464104|lt=Akiko Yosano|1=Yosano Akiko}} were captivated by the harmony of hot springs and dramatic valleys. The area was then known as Yuhara Onsen, a name still in occasional use. The opening of the {{ill|Jōetsu Line|en|Jōetsu Line|qid=Q1135061|lt=Jōetsu Line|1=Jōetsu Line}} in 1931 brought Minakami Onsen closer to the {{ill|Capital region|en|Capital region|qid=Q1062177|lt=Greater Tokyo Area|1=Capital region}}. The 1966 {{ill|Kikufuji Hotel fire|ja|菊富士ホテル火災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikufuji Hotel fire|1=Q11619620|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11619620}} marked a significant event in its history. The construction of the {{ill|Kan-etsu Expressway|en|Kan-etsu Expressway|qid=Q1059893|lt=Kan-Etsu Expressway|1=Kan-etsu Expressway}} and the introduction of express trains like the {{ill|Yukemuri Express|ja|水上列車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukemuri Express|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} led to its rise as a major recreation destination. However, the shift towards group tours and nightlife transformed the onsen into a bustling entertainment hub, complete with bars and show pubs. Following the {{ill|Lost Decades|en|Lost Decades|qid=Q1141122|lt=economic bubble collapse|1=Lost Decades}}, declining group tours and changing tourist preferences forced the onsen to refocus. Today, it aims to leverage its natural surroundings, integrating {{ill|Skiing|en|Skiing|qid=Q130949|lt=skiing|1=Skiing}}, {{ill|Rafting|en|Rafting|qid=Q207238|lt=rafting|1=Rafting}}, and other outdoor activities to develop as a recreational resort. == Recent Issues == The {{ill|Ōshimizu Tunnel|ja|大清水トンネル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōshimizu Tunnel|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 清水トンネル|1=Shimizu Tunnels|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q75220055}} of the {{ill|Jōetsu Shinkansen|en|Jōetsu Shinkansen|qid=Q912219|lt=Jōetsu Shinkansen|1=Jōetsu Shinkansen}} passes 50 meters beneath the hot spring's aquifers, causing decreased water flow. In 1979, the issue was addressed in the {{ill|House of Representatives (Japan)|en|House of Representatives (Japan)|qid=Q659587|lt=House of Representatives of Japan|1=House of Representatives (Japan)}}, prompting the {{ill|Japan Railway Construction Public Corporation|en|Japan Railway Construction Public Corporation|qid=Q6157806|lt=Japan Railway Construction Public Corporation|1=Japan Railway Construction Public Corporation}} to drill new wells. The 2004 {{ill|onsen fraud scandal|ja|温泉偽装問題|1=Onsen Fraud Controversy|qq=Onsen Fraud Scandal|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/onsen fraud scandal|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Fraud Controversy|qq=}} revealed that some facilities used tap water while advertising natural hot springs, raising concerns over misrepresentation and improper tax collection. The {{ill|abandoned Hotel Ōmiya|ja|ホテル大宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/abandoned Hotel Ōmiya|1=Abandoned Hotel Ōmiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q85873736}}, located near Minakami Station, remains an unresolved issue for the local community. == Access == * Train: {{ill|Jōetsu Line|en|Jōetsu Line|qid=Q1135061|lt=Jōetsu Line|1=Jōetsu Line}}, Minakami Station, approximately 5 minutes on foot * Car: Exit at Minakami Interchange on the {{ill|Kan-etsu Expressway|en|Kan-etsu Expressway|qid=Q1059893|lt=Kan-Etsu Expressway|1=Kan-etsu Expressway}}, via National Route 291 == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * ''Disappearing Ruins of Japan: Hidden History Through Ruins,'' Taiyo Publishing, 2014 == See Also == * {{ill|Ii Yudana|ja|いい湯だな|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Yudana|1=Q11259008|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11259008}} (song by {{ill|The Dukes Aces|ja|デューク・エイセス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Dukes Aces|1=Duke Aces|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11320788}}) - Features Minakami in the fourth verse == External Links == * [https://www.enjoy-minakami.jp/ Minakami Perfect Guide] - Official tourism website of Minakami {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|水上温泉|version=95165560|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:水上温泉]] {{Commons category|Minakami Onsen}} {{wikidata link|Q11548228}} [[Category:Copper-Iron Spring]] [[Category:Hot springs of Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Sulfate spring]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
